11: ---
12: 
13: # JavaScript Style Guide
14: 
15: Formatting standards and naming conventions for JavaScript files in the OpenCode development environment.
16: 
17: ---
18: 
19: ## 1. OVERVIEW
20: 
21: ### Purpose
22: 
23: Defines consistent styling rules for JavaScript files to ensure readability, maintainability, and alignment across all OpenCode JavaScript code.
24: 
25: ### When to Use
26: 
27: - Writing new JavaScript files
28: - Reviewing JavaScript code for consistency
29: - Resolving style disagreements in code review
30: 
31: ---
32: 
33: ## 2. FILE HEADER FORMAT
34: 
35: All JavaScript files MUST begin with a boxed header identifying the module.
36: For `.mjs` files and OpenCode plugin loader paths, keep the same header style
37: but follow ESM module syntax instead of CommonJS.
38: 
39: ### Template
40: 
41: ```javascript
42: // ╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
43: // ║ [Module Name]                                                             ║
44: // ╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
45: ```
46: 
47: ### Requirements
48: 
49: - Box width: 78 characters total
50: - Module name: Centered or left-aligned within box
51: - Immediately followed by `'use strict';` directive for `.js/.cjs` files
52: - `.mjs` and plugin ESM files do not require `'use strict'`
53: 
54: **Evidence**: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/utils/logger.ts:1-3`,
55: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/errors/core.ts:1-3`
56: 
57: ---
58: 
59: ## 3. USE STRICT DIRECTIVE
60: 
61: Every `.js` and `.cjs` JavaScript file MUST include the strict mode directive.
62: Do not add it to `.mjs` files; ES modules are strict by definition and the
63: alignment verifier intentionally skips `.mjs` strict-mode enforcement.
64: 
65: ```javascript
66: 'use strict';
67: ```
68: 
69: **Placement**: Immediately after file header, before any other code.
70: 
71: **Evidence**: `verify_alignment_drift.py` skips `.mjs`; CommonJS-style JS
72: helpers still use `'use strict'`.
73: 
74: ---
75: 
76: ## 4. SECTION ORGANIZATION
77: 
78: Large files are organized using numbered section dividers.
79: 
80: ### Section Divider Template
81: 
82: ```javascript
83: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
84: // 1. [SECTION NAME]
85: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
86: ```
87: 
88: ### Standard Section Order
89: 
90: | Order | Section Name     | Purpose                           |
91: |-------|------------------|-----------------------------------|
92: | 1     | IMPORTS/REQUIRES | Module dependencies               |
93: | 2     | CONSTANTS        | Configuration values, magic numbers |
94: | 3     | HELPERS          | Internal utility functions        |
95: | 4     | CORE LOGIC       | Main implementation               |
96: | 5     | EXPORTS          | Module public interface           |
97: 
98: **Evidence**: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/core/config.ts`
99: 
100: ---
101: 
102: ## 5. NAMING CONVENTIONS
103: 
104: ### Function Names
105: 
106: **Style**: `camelCase`
107: 
108: ```javascript
109: // CORRECT
110: function loadConfig(path) { }
111: function memorySearch(query, options) { }
112: function validateInput(data) { }
113: 
114: // INCORRECT
115: function load_config(path) { }     // snake_case
116: function LoadConfig(path) { }      // PascalCase
117: function load-config(path) { }     // kebab-case (invalid syntax)
118: ```
119: 
120: **Evidence**: MDN Web Docs, Airbnb Style Guide, Node.js core APIs
121: 
122: ### Constant Names
123: 
124: **Style**: `UPPER_SNAKE_CASE`
125: 
126: ```javascript
127: // CORRECT
128: const MAX_RETRIES = 3;
129: const DEFAULT_TIMEOUT = 5000;
130: const SQLITE_BUSY_TIMEOUT = 30000;
131: 
132: // INCORRECT
133: const maxRetries = 3;       // camelCase
134: const max_retries = 3;      // snake_case (reserved for variables)
135: ```
136: 
137: **Evidence**: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/core/config.ts`
138: 
139: ### Class Names
140: 
141: **Style**: `PascalCase`
142: 
143: ```javascript
144: // CORRECT
145: class MemoryError extends Error { }
146: class ConfigLoader { }
147: class VectorIndex { }
148: 
149: // INCORRECT
150: class memoryError { }       // camelCase
151: class memory_error { }      // snake_case
152: ```
153: 
154: **Evidence**: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/errors/core.ts`
155: 
156: ### Variable Names
157: 
158: | Scope        | Style        | Example                          |
159: |--------------|--------------|----------------------------------|
160: | Local        | `camelCase`  | `const searchResults = []`       |
161: | Module-level | `camelCase`  | `const dbPath = '...'`           |
162: | Parameters   | `camelCase`  | `function search(query, maxResults)` |
163: 
164: ### Boolean Names
165: 
166: **Style**: `camelCase` with `is`/`has`/`can`/`should` prefix
167: 
168: ```javascript
169: // CORRECT
170: const isValid = true;
171: const hasResults = items.length > 0;
172: const canProceed = !isBlocked;
173: const shouldRetry = attempts < MAX_RETRIES;
174: 
175: // INCORRECT
176: const is_valid = true;       // snake_case
177: const valid = true;          // ambiguous
178: ```
179: 
180: ### Naming Summary Table
181: 
182: | Element          | Convention         | Example                |
183: |------------------|--------------------|------------------------|
184: | Functions        | `camelCase`        | `loadConfig`           |
185: | Constants        | `UPPER_SNAKE_CASE` | `MAX_RETRIES`          |
186: | Classes          | `PascalCase`       | `MemoryError`          |
187: | Local variables  | `camelCase`        | `searchResults`        |
188: | Module variables | `camelCase`        | `dbConnection`         |
189: | Parameters       | `camelCase`        | `queryText`            |
190: | Booleans         | `is`/`has`/`can`   | `isValid`, `hasItems`  |
191: 
192: ---
193: 
194: ## 6. FORMATTING RULES
195: 
196: ### Indentation
197: 
198: - **Size**: 2 spaces
199: - **Tabs**: Never use tabs
200: 
201: ```javascript
202: // CORRECT (2 spaces)
203: function example() {
204:   if (condition) {
205:     return value;
206:   }
207: }
208: 
209: // INCORRECT (4 spaces or tabs)
210: function example() {
211:     if (condition) {
212:         return value;
213:     }
214: }
215: ```
216: 
217: ### Braces
218: 
219: **Style**: K&R (opening brace on same line)
220: 
221: ```javascript
222: // CORRECT (K&R style)
223: if (condition) {
224:   // code
225: } else {
226:   // code
227: }
228: 
229: function example() {
230:   // code
231: }
232: 
233: // INCORRECT (Allman style)
234: if (condition)
235: {
236:   // code
237: }
238: ```
239: 
240: ### Semicolons
241: 
242: **Rule**: Always use semicolons
243: 
244: ```javascript
245: // CORRECT
246: const value = 42;
247: return result;
248: 
249: // INCORRECT (ASI-dependent)
250: const value = 42
251: return result
252: ```
253: 
254: ### Quotes
255: 
256: **Rule**: Single quotes for strings
257: 
258: ```javascript
259: // CORRECT

1: ---
2: title: JavaScript Quality Standards
3: description: Module organization, error handling, documentation, and security patterns for JavaScript files.
4: trigger_phrases:
5:   - "opencode javascript quality standards"
6:   - "javascript module organization"
7:   - "commonjs export pattern"
8:   - "javascript error handling standards"
9: importance_tier: normal
10: contextType: implementation
11: ---
12: 
13: # JavaScript Quality Standards
14: 
15: Module organization, error handling, documentation, and security patterns for JavaScript files.
16: 
17: ---
18: 
19: ## 1. OVERVIEW
20: 
21: ### Purpose
22: 
23: Establishes module organization, error handling, documentation, and security patterns that all JavaScript code must follow.
24: 
25: ### When to Use
26: 
27: - Writing new JavaScript modules
28: - Reviewing code for quality compliance
29: - Setting up error handling and security patterns
30: 
31: ---
32: 
33: ## 2. MODULE ORGANIZATION
34: 
35: ### Module-System Boundary
36: 
37: Use the module format required by the file's package boundary and loader
38: contract. Do not force one JavaScript module style across all paths.
39: 
40: | Surface | Module Pattern | Notes |
41: |---------|----------------|-------|
42: | `.js/.cjs` Node utilities outside plugin loader paths | CommonJS with `'use strict'` | Default for legacy runtime helpers and standalone scripts |
43: | `.mjs` files | ESM | The verifier skips `'use strict'` enforcement for `.mjs` |
44: | `.opencode/plugins/*.{js,mjs,ts}` | ESM default export | Required by the OpenCode plugin loader |
45: | `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/plugin_bridges/*.{js,mjs,ts}` | ESM default export | Bridge helpers follow the plugin-loader contract |
46: 
47: ### CommonJS Pattern
48: 
49: Non-plugin `.js/.cjs` modules use CommonJS format with `'use strict'`
50: directive.
51: 
52: ```javascript
53: 'use strict';
54: 
55: // ... imports and implementation ...
56: 
57: module.exports = {
58:   // Exports here
59: };
60: ```
61: 
62: **Evidence**: Current TypeScript packages are ESM/NodeNext; this CommonJS
63: pattern applies to legacy `.js/.cjs` utility surfaces and test runners that use
64: `require`.
65: 
66: ### Export Pattern
67: 
68: Export functions using `camelCase` names. For MCP handlers, include
69: `snake_case` aliases for backward compatibility. Do not apply this export shape
70: to OpenCode plugin paths; those use `export default`.
71: 
72: ```javascript
73: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
74: // 5. EXPORTS
75: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
76: 
77: module.exports = {
78:   // Primary exports (camelCase)
79:   loadConfig,
80:   memorySearch,
81:   validateInput,
82: 
83:   // Backward-compatible aliases (snake_case) — MCP handlers only
84:   load_config: loadConfig,
85:   memory_search: memorySearch,
86:   validate_input: validateInput
87: };
88: ```
89: 
90: **Evidence**: Legacy CommonJS export examples are historical; current
91: system-spec-kit TypeScript source uses ESM `export` statements and compiles per
92: workspace package settings.
93: 
94: ### Barrel Exports
95: 
96: Index files re-export from multiple modules.
97: 
98: ```javascript
99: // index.js
100: 'use strict';
101: 
102: module.exports = {
103:   ...require('./config'),
104:   ...require('./database'),
105:   ...require('./search')
106: };
107: ```
108: 
109: ---
110: 
111: ## 3. ERROR HANDLING
112: 
113: ### Guard Clause Pattern
114: 
115: Validate inputs at function start. Return early on failure.
116: 
117: ```javascript
118: function processData(input, options) {
119:   // Guard clauses first
120:   if (!input || typeof input !== 'string') {
121:     return { success: false, error: 'Invalid input: expected string' };
122:   }
123: 
124:   if (!options || typeof options !== 'object') {
125:     return { success: false, error: 'Invalid options: expected object' };
126:   }
127: 
128:   // Main logic after guards pass
129:   const result = transform(input, options);
130:   return { success: true, data: result };
131: }
132: ```
133: 
134: **Evidence**: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts`
135: 
136: ### Try-Catch Pattern
137: 
138: Wrap async operations with specific error handling.
139: 
140: ```javascript
141: async function fetchData(query) {
142:   try {
143:     const result = await database.query(query);
144:     return { success: true, data: result };
145:   } catch (error) {
146:     logger.error(`[fetchData] Query failed: ${error.message}`);
147:     return { success: false, error: error.message };
148:   }
149: }
150: ```
151: 
152: **Evidence**: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts`
153: 
154: ### Custom Error Classes
155: 
156: Extend Error for domain-specific errors.
157: 
158: ```javascript
159: /**
160:  * Custom error for memory operations.
161:  */
162: class MemoryError extends Error {
163:   /**
164:    * Create a MemoryError.
165:    * @param {string} message - Error description
166:    * @param {string} code - Error code (e.g., 'DB_CONNECTION_FAILED')
167:    * @param {Object} [context] - Additional context
168:    */
169:   constructor(message, code, context = {}) {
170:     super(message);
171:     this.name = 'MemoryError';
172:     this.code = code;
173:     this.context = context;
174:     Error.captureStackTrace(this, this.constructor);
175:   }
176: }
177: 
178: module.exports = { MemoryError };
179: ```
180: 
181: **Evidence**: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/errors/core.ts`
182: 
183: ### Error Response Pattern
184: 
185: Consistent error response structure.
186: 
187: ```javascript
188: // Success response
189: { success: true, data: result }
190: 
191: // Error response
192: { success: false, error: 'Error message', code: 'ERROR_CODE' }
193: ```
194: 
195: ---
196: 
197: ## 4. CONSOLE LOGGING
198: 
199: ### Bracketed Module Prefix
200: 
201: All console output uses bracketed module identifier.
202: 
203: ```javascript
204: // Format: [module-name] Message
205: console.log(`[context-server] Server started on port ${port}`);
206: console.error(`[memory-search] Search failed: ${error.message}`);
207: console.warn(`[config] Using default value for missing key: ${key}`);
208: ```
209: 
210: **Evidence**: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts`
211: 
212: ### Log Levels
213: 
214: | Level   | Usage                            | Example                                    |
215: |---------|----------------------------------|--------------------------------------------|
216: | `log`   | General information              | `[server] Request received`                |
217: | `error` | Errors requiring attention       | `[db] Connection failed: timeout`          |
218: | `warn`  | Potential issues, fallbacks      | `[config] Missing value, using default`    |
219: | `debug` | Development-only verbose output  | `[search] Query plan: ${JSON.stringify()}` |
220: 
221: ---
222: 
223: ## 5. JSDOC DOCUMENTATION
224: 
225: ### Function Documentation
226: 
227: Required for all exported functions.
228: 
229: ```javascript
230: /**
231:  * Search memory database for matching entries.
232:  *
233:  * @param {string} query - Search query text
234:  * @param {Object} options - Search configuration
235:  * @param {number} [options.limit=10] - Maximum results to return
236:  * @param {string} [options.specFolder] - Filter by spec folder path
237:  * @param {string[]} [options.anchors] - Filter by anchor types
238:  * @returns {Promise<Array<Object>>} Array of matching memory entries
239:  * @throws {MemoryError} If database connection fails
240:  *
241:  * @example
242:  * const results = await memorySearch('authentication', {
243:  *   limit: 5,
244:  *   specFolder: 'specs/007-auth'
245:  * });
246:  */
247: async function memorySearch(query, options = {}) {
248:   // implementation
249: }
250: ```
251: 
252: **Evidence**: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts`
253: 
254: ### Type Annotations
255: 
256: Common JSDoc type patterns.
257: 
258: ```javascript
259: /**
260:  * @param {string} name - Required string parameter

(Showing lines 1-260 of 591. Use offset=261 to continue.)
</content>

1: ---
2: title: TypeScript Quality Standards
3: description: Type system patterns, error handling, documentation, async patterns, and tsconfig baseline for TypeScript files in the OpenCode development environment.
4: trigger_phrases:
5:   - "opencode typescript quality standards"
6:   - "interface vs type decision"
7:   - "typescript type safety policies"
8:   - "tsconfig baseline standards"
9: importance_tier: normal
10: contextType: implementation
11: ---
12: 
13: # TypeScript Quality Standards
14: 
15: Type system patterns, error handling, documentation, async patterns, and tsconfig baseline for TypeScript files in the OpenCode development environment.
16: 
17: ---
18: 
19: ## 1. OVERVIEW
20: 
21: ### Purpose
22: 
23: Establishes type system patterns, error handling, documentation, async patterns, and tsconfig baseline that all TypeScript code in the OpenCode development environment must follow.
24: 
25: ### When to Use
26: 
27: - Writing new TypeScript modules
28: - Reviewing TypeScript code for quality compliance
29: - Setting up type safety, error handling, and documentation patterns
30: 
31: ---
32: 
33: ## 2. INTERFACE vs TYPE DECISION GUIDE
34: 
35: ### When to Use `interface`
36: 
37: Use `interface` for object shapes, class contracts, and data structures:
38: 
39: ```typescript
40: // Object shapes
41: interface SearchResult {
42:   readonly id: string;
43:   score: number;
44:   text: string;
45:   metadata: Record<string, unknown>;
46: }
47: 
48: // Class contracts
49: interface EmbeddingProvider {
50:   embed(text: string): Promise<number[]>;
51:   batchEmbed(texts: string[]): Promise<number[][]>;
52:   getDimension(): number;
53:   getModelName(): string;
54:   isReady(): boolean;
55: }
56: 
57: // Extending shapes
58: interface DetailedSearchResult extends SearchResult {
59:   highlights: string[];
60:   explanation: string;
61: }
62: ```
63: 
64: **Why interfaces for objects**: Interfaces support declaration merging, provide clearer error messages, and signal "this is a data contract" to the reader.
65: 
66: ### When to Use `type`
67: 
68: Use `type` for unions, intersections, mapped types, conditional types, and function signatures:
69: 
70: ```typescript
71: // Union types
72: type MemoryState = 'active' | 'archived' | 'pending' | 'deleted';
73: 
74: // Intersection types
75: type Timestamped<T> = T & { createdAt: number; updatedAt: number };
76: 
77: // Function signatures
78: type ScoreCalculator = (input: number, weight: number) => number;
79: 
80: // Conditional types
81: type AsyncReturn<T> = T extends Promise<infer U> ? U : T;
82: 
83: // Mapped types
84: type Nullable<T> = { [K in keyof T]: T[K] | null };
85: 
86: // Tuple types
87: type Coordinates = [x: number, y: number];
88: ```
89: 
90: ### Decision Summary
91: 
92: | Use Case                           | Construct   | Reason                          |
93: |------------------------------------|-------------|---------------------------------|
94: | Object shapes / data structures    | `interface` | Extensible, clear error messages|
95: | Class contracts                    | `interface` | Supports `implements`           |
96: | Union types                        | `type`      | Only `type` supports unions     |
97: | Intersection / mapped / conditional| `type`      | Only `type` supports these      |
98: | Function signatures (standalone)   | `type`      | Cleaner syntax than interface    |
99: | Simple primitives / literals       | `type`      | `type ID = string`              |
100: 
101: ---
102: 
103: ## 3. TYPE SAFETY POLICIES
104: 
105: ### `unknown` Over `any`
106: 
107: **Policy**: Use `unknown` instead of `any` in public API surfaces. The `any` type disables type checking entirely. `unknown` forces callers to narrow the type before use.
108: 
109: ```typescript
110: // CORRECT — unknown forces type narrowing
111: function parseInput(raw: unknown): SearchConfig {
112:   if (typeof raw !== 'object' || raw === null) {
113:     throw new TypeError('Expected object input');
114:   }
115:   const obj = raw as Record<string, unknown>;
116:   // validate and narrow further...
117:   return obj as SearchConfig;
118: }
119: 
120: // INCORRECT — any disables all type checking
121: function parseInput(raw: any): SearchConfig {
122:   return raw;  // No validation, no safety
123: }
124: ```
125: 
126: **Permitted `any` usage** (must include justification comment):
127: 
128: ```typescript
129: // Interop with untyped third-party library (sqlite-vec native module)
130: // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
131: const sqliteVec = require('sqlite-vec') as any;
132: ```
133: 
134: ### Strict Null Checks
135: 
136: With `strictNullChecks: true`, `null` and `undefined` must be handled explicitly:
137: 
138: ```typescript
139: // CORRECT — explicit null handling
140: function findMemory(id: string): MemoryRecord | null {
141:   const record = database.get(id);
142:   if (!record) return null;
143:   return record;
144: }
145: 
146: // Usage requires null check
147: const memory = findMemory('abc');
148: if (memory !== null) {
149:   console.log(memory.text);  // TypeScript knows memory is not null here
150: }
151: ```
152: 
153: ### Non-Null Assertions
154: 
155: **Policy**: Avoid non-null assertions (`!`). When absolutely necessary, include a justification comment:
156: 
157: ```typescript
158: // AVOID — non-null assertion hides potential bugs
159: const name = user!.name;
160: 
161: // PREFERRED — explicit null check
162: if (user === null) throw new Error('User not found');
163: const name = user.name;
164: 
165: // PERMITTED — with justification
166: // Process guarantees element exists after validation phase (see line 42)
167: const element = document.getElementById('root')!;
168: ```
169: 
170: ### Generic Constraints
171: 
172: Use `extends` to constrain generic type parameters:
173: 
174: ```typescript
175: // CORRECT — constrained generic
176: function getProperty<T extends Record<string, unknown>, K extends keyof T>(
177:   obj: T,
178:   key: K,
179: ): T[K] {
180:   return obj[key];
181: }
182: 
183: // CORRECT — constraint ensures required shape
184: interface HasId { readonly id: string; }
185: function findById<T extends HasId>(items: T[], id: string): T | undefined {
186:   return items.find(item => item.id === id);
187: }
188: 
189: // INCORRECT — unconstrained when constraint is needed
190: function getProperty<T>(obj: T, key: string): unknown {
191:   return (obj as Record<string, unknown>)[key];  // Loses type safety
192: }
193: ```
194: 
195: ---
196: 
197: ## 4. DISCRIMINATED UNIONS
198: 
199: Use discriminated unions for state management where an object can be in one of several distinct states:
200: 
201: ```typescript
202: // Discriminated union for operation results
203: interface SuccessResult<T> {
204:   readonly success: true;
205:   readonly data: T;
206: }
207: 
208: interface ErrorResult {
209:   readonly success: false;
210:   readonly error: string;
211:   readonly code: ErrorCode;
212: }
213: 
214: type OperationResult<T> = SuccessResult<T> | ErrorResult;
215: 
216: // Usage — TypeScript narrows automatically
217: function handleResult(result: OperationResult<SearchResult[]>): void {
218:   if (result.success) {
219:     // TypeScript knows: result.data exists
220:     console.log(`Found ${result.data.length} results`);
221:   } else {
222:     // TypeScript knows: result.error and result.code exist
223:     console.error(`Error ${result.code}: ${result.error}`);
224:   }
225: }
226: ```
227: 
228: ### State Machine Pattern
229: 
230: ```typescript
231: type ConnectionState =
232:   | { status: 'disconnected' }
233:   | { status: 'connecting'; attempt: number }
234:   | { status: 'connected'; connection: DatabaseConnection }
235:   | { status: 'error'; error: Error; lastAttempt: number };
236: 
237: function getConnectionInfo(state: ConnectionState): string {
238:   switch (state.status) {
239:     case 'disconnected':
240:       return 'Not connected';
241:     case 'connecting':
242:       return `Connecting (attempt ${state.attempt})`;
243:     case 'connected':
244:       return `Connected to ${state.connection.host}`;
245:     case 'error':
246:       return `Error: ${state.error.message}`;
247:   }
248: }
249: ```
250: 
251: ---
252: 
253: ## 5. UTILITY TYPES
254: 
255: ### Standard TypeScript Utility Types
256: 
257: Use built-in utility types instead of reinventing them:
258: 
259: | Utility Type       | Purpose                                    | Example                                     |
260: |--------------------|--------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------|

(Showing lines 1-260 of 824. Use offset=261 to continue.)
</content>

1: ---
2: title: Shell Style Guide
3: description: Coding conventions and formatting standards for Bash scripts in the OpenCode development environment.
4: trigger_phrases:
5:   - "opencode shell style guide"
6:   - "bash formatting standards"
7:   - "shell shebang header"
8:   - "bash naming conventions"
9: importance_tier: normal
10: contextType: implementation
11: ---
12: 
13: # Shell Style Guide
14: 
15: Coding conventions and formatting standards for Bash scripts in the OpenCode development environment.
16: 
17: ---
18: 
19: ## 1. OVERVIEW
20: 
21: ### Purpose
22: 
23: Defines consistent styling rules for Bash scripts to ensure reliability, readability, and maintainability across all OpenCode shell scripts.
24: 
25: ### Scope
26: 
27: Applies to all shell files in:
28: - `.opencode/skills/*/scripts/` - Skill automation scripts
29: - `.opencode/agents/scripts/` - Agent provider scripts
30: - `scripts/` - Build and deployment scripts
31: 
32: ### Key Sources
33: 
34: | File | Evidence |
35: |------|----------|
36: | `lib/common.sh` | Color definitions, logging functions, utilities |
37: | `spec/create.sh` | Strict mode, argument parsing, control flow |
38: 
39: ---
40: 
41: ## 2. FILE STRUCTURE
42: 
43: ### Shebang Line
44: 
45: **ALWAYS** use the portable bash shebang:
46: 
47: ```bash
48: #!/usr/bin/env bash
49: ```
50: 
51: **Evidence**: `lib/common.sh:1`
52: 
53: ### File Header
54: 
55: Use the standard header format for identification:
56: 
57: ```bash
58: #!/usr/bin/env bash
59: # ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
60: # COMPONENT: [COMPONENT NAME]
61: # ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
62: # Brief description of what this script does.
63: #
64: # Usage: script.sh [options] <arguments>
65: ```
66: 
67: **Alternative** (detailed header for major scripts):
68: 
69: ```bash
70: #!/usr/bin/env bash
71: # ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
72: # SPECKIT: CREATE SPEC FOLDER
73: # ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
74: # Creates spec folder with templates based on documentation level.
75: #
76: # TEMPLATE ARCHITECTURE (v2.0 - CORE + ADDENDUM):
77: #   templates/
78: #   ├── level_1/        # Core only (~270 LOC total)
79: #   ├── level_2/        # Core + Verification (~390 LOC)
80: #   └── level_3/        # Core + Verification + Architecture (~540 LOC)
81: ```
82: 
83: **Evidence**: `spec/create.sh:1-20`
84: 
85: ### Strict Mode
86: 
87: **ALWAYS** enable strict mode immediately after the shebang/header:
88: 
89: ```bash
90: set -euo pipefail
91: ```
92: 
93: | Flag | Purpose |
94: |------|---------|
95: | `-e` | Exit on error (non-zero return) |
96: | `-u` | Error on undefined variables |
97: | `-o pipefail` | Pipe fails if any command fails |
98: 
99: **Evidence**: `spec/create.sh:22`
100: 
101: ### Section Organization
102: 
103: Organize code into numbered sections:
104: 
105: ```bash
106: # ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
107: # 1. COLOR DEFINITIONS
108: # ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
109: 
110: RED='\033[0;31m'
111: GREEN='\033[0;32m'
112: NC='\033[0m'
113: 
114: # ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
115: # 2. UTILITY FUNCTIONS
116: # ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
117: 
118: log_info() {
119:     printf "${BLUE}INFO:${NC} %s\n" "$1"
120: }
121: ```
122: 
123: **Evidence**: `lib/common.sh:9-35`
124: 
125: ---
126: 
127: ## 3. NAMING CONVENTIONS
128: 
129: ### Functions
130: 
131: **Use**: `lowercase_with_underscores`
132: 
133: ```bash
134: # Public functions
135: validate_input() {
136:     local input="$1"
137:     # ...
138: }
139: 
140: process_file() {
141:     local file_path="$1"
142:     # ...
143: }
144: ```
145: 
146: ### Private/Internal Functions
147: 
148: **Use**: Single underscore prefix for helpers:
149: 
150: ```bash
151: # Internal helper function
152: _json_escape() {
153:     local str="$1"
154:     str="${str//\\/\\\\}"
155:     str="${str//\"/\\\"}"
156:     printf '%s' "$str"
157: }
158: ```
159: 
160: **Evidence**: `lib/common.sh:37-45`
161: 
162: ### Variables
163: 
164: **Use**: `lowercase_with_underscores` for local variables:
165: 
166: ```bash
167: local file_path="$1"
168: local output_dir="${2:-./output}"
169: local is_valid=true
170: ```
171: 
172: ### Constants and Global Variables
173: 
174: **Use**: `UPPER_SNAKE_CASE` for constants and script-level variables:
175: 
176: ```bash
177: readonly MAX_RETRIES=3
178: readonly DEFAULT_TIMEOUT=30
179: 
180: # Script-level state
181: ERRORS=0
182: WARNINGS=0
183: JSON_MODE=false
184: ```
185:

# Shell Style Guide

Coding conventions and formatting standards for Bash scripts in the OpenCode development environment.

---

## 1. OVERVIEW

### Purpose

Defines consistent styling rules for Bash scripts to ensure reliability, readability, and maintainability across all OpenCode shell scripts.

### Scope

Applies to all shell files in:
- `.opencode/skills/*/scripts/` - Skill automation scripts
- `.opencode/agents/scripts/` - Agent provider scripts
- `scripts/` - Build and deployment scripts

### Key Sources

| File | Evidence |
|------|----------|
| `lib/common.sh` | Color definitions, logging functions, utilities |
| `spec/create.sh` | Strict mode, argument parsing, control flow |

---

## 2. FILE STRUCTURE

### Shebang Line

**ALWAYS** use the portable bash shebang:

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
```

**Evidence**: `lib/common.sh:1`

### File Header

Use the standard header format for identification:

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# COMPONENT: [COMPONENT NAME]
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Brief description of what this script does.
#
# Usage: script.sh [options] <arguments>
```

**Alternative** (detailed header for major scripts):

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# SPECKIT: CREATE SPEC FOLDER
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Creates spec folder with templates based on documentation level.
#
# TEMPLATE ARCHITECTURE (v2.0 - CORE + ADDENDUM):
#   templates/
#   ├── level_1/        # Core only (~270 LOC total)
#   ├── level_2/        # Core + Verification (~390 LOC)
#   └── level_3/        # Core + Verification + Architecture (~540 LOC)
```

**Evidence**: `spec/create.sh:1-20`

### Strict Mode

**ALWAYS** enable strict mode immediately after the shebang/header:

```bash
set -euo pipefail
```

| Flag | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `-e` | Exit on error (non-zero return) |
| `-u` | Error on undefined variables |
| `-o pipefail` | Pipe fails if any command fails |

**Evidence**: `spec/create.sh:22`

### Section Organization

Organize code into numbered sections:

```bash
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# 1. COLOR DEFINITIONS
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
NC='\033[0m'

# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# 2. UTILITY FUNCTIONS
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

log_info() {
    printf "${BLUE}INFO:${NC} %s\n" "$1"
}
```

**Evidence**: `lib/common.sh:9-35`

---

## 3. NAMING CONVENTIONS

### Functions

**Use**: `lowercase_with_underscores`

```bash
# Public functions
validate_input() {
    local input="$1"
    # ...
}

process_file() {
    local file_path="$1"
    # ...
}
```

### Private/Internal Functions

**Use**: Single underscore prefix for helpers:

```bash
# Internal helper function
_json_escape() {
    local str="$1"
    str="${str//\\/\\\\}"
    str="${str//\"/\\\"}"
    printf '%s' "$str"
}
```

**Evidence**: `lib/common.sh:37-45`

### Variables

**Use**: `lowercase_with_underscores` for local variables:

```bash
local file_path="$1"
local output_dir="${2:-./output}"
local is_valid=true
```

### Constants and Global Variables

**Use**: `UPPER_SNAKE_CASE` for constants and script-level variables:

```bash
readonly MAX_RETRIES=3
readonly DEFAULT_TIMEOUT=30

# Script-level state
ERRORS=0
WARNINGS=0
JSON_MODE=false
```

**Evidence**: `lib/common.sh:28-31`, `spec/create.sh:24-33`

---

## 4. COLOR DEFINITIONS

### Standard Color Setup

Define colors with terminal detection:

```bash
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# COLOR DEFINITIONS
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
BLUE='\033[0;34m'
BOLD='\033[1m'
NC='\033[0m'  # No Color / Reset

# Disable colors if not terminal (for piping/logging)
if [[ ! -t 1 ]]; then
    RED='' GREEN='' YELLOW='' BLUE='' BOLD='' NC=''
fi
```

**Evidence**: `lib/common.sh:13-22`

### Usage in Output

```bash
# Colored output
printf "${GREEN}SUCCESS:${NC} %s\n" "$message"

# Python Style Guide

Coding conventions and formatting standards for Python scripts in the OpenCode development environment.

---

## 1. OVERVIEW

### Purpose

Defines consistent styling rules for Python code to ensure readability, maintainability, and alignment across all OpenCode Python scripts.

### Scope

Applies to all Python files in:
- `.opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/scripts/` - Skill routing scripts
- `.opencode/skills/*/scripts/` - Skill-specific scripts
- `scripts/` - Project-level automation

### Key Sources

| File | Evidence |
|------|----------|
| `.opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/scripts/skill_advisor.py` | Header format, naming conventions, docstrings |
| `.opencode/skills/sk-doc/scripts/package_skill.py` | Function structure, early returns, type hints |

---

## 2. FILE STRUCTURE

### Shebang Line

**ALWAYS** start Python scripts with the portable shebang:

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
```

**Evidence**: `.opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/scripts/skill_advisor.py:1`

### File Header

Use the box-style header for component identification:

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# COMPONENT: [COMPONENT NAME]
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
```

**Alternative** (full box for major scripts):

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# ╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
# ║ [Script Name]                                                             ║
# ╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
```

**Evidence**: `.opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/scripts/skill_advisor.py:1-4`

### Module Docstring

Place immediately after the header, before imports:

```python
"""
Brief description of what this module does.

Usage: python script_name.py [arguments]
Output: Description of output format

Options:
    --flag      Description of flag
    --option    Description of option
"""
```

**Evidence**: `.opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/scripts/skill_advisor.py:6-16`

### Import Order

Follow PEP 8 import ordering:

```python
# 1. Standard library imports
import sys
import json
import os
import re
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Tuple, List, Dict, Optional

# 2. Third-party imports (if any)
import yaml

# 3. Local imports (if any)
from .utils import helper_function
```

**Evidence**: `.opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/scripts/skill_advisor.py:17-22`, `.opencode/skills/sk-doc/scripts/package_skill.py:25-30`

### Section Comments

Organize code into logical sections using consistent dividers:

```python
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# 1. CONFIGURATION
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

CONSTANT_VALUE = "value"

# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# 2. VALIDATION FUNCTIONS
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def validate_something():
    pass
```

**Evidence**: `.opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/scripts/skill_advisor.py:25-27`, `.opencode/skills/sk-doc/scripts/package_skill.py:32-34`

---

## 3. NAMING CONVENTIONS

### Functions and Variables

**Use**: `snake_case` (lowercase with underscores)

```python
# Functions
def validate_frontmatter(content: str) -> Tuple[bool, str]:
    pass

def get_script_dir() -> Path:
    pass

# Variables
user_request = "input"
file_path = Path("/some/path")
is_valid = True
```

**Evidence**: `.opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/scripts/skill_advisor.py`, `.opencode/skills/sk-doc/scripts/package_skill.py:87`

### Classes

**Use**: `PascalCase`

```python
class SkillValidator:
    pass

class ValidationResult:
    pass
```

### Constants

**Use**: `UPPER_SNAKE_CASE`

```python
STOP_WORDS = {'a', 'an', 'the'}
REQUIRED_FRONTMATTER_FIELDS = ['name', 'description']
MAX_SKILL_MD_WORDS = 5000
```

**Evidence**: `.opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/scripts/skill_advisor.py:31-49`, `.opencode/skills/sk-doc/scripts/package_skill.py:37-81`

### Private Functions

**Use**: Single leading underscore for internal/helper functions:

```python
def _parse_frontmatter(content: str) -> dict:
    """Internal helper - not part of public API."""
    pass
```

---

## 4. TYPE HINTS

### Function Signatures

**ALWAYS** include type hints for function parameters and return values:

```python
def validate_config(config_path: Path) -> Tuple[bool, str, List[str]]:
    """Validate configuration file."""
    pass

def process_data(data: Dict[str, Any], strict: bool = True) -> Optional[str]:
    """Process data with optional strict mode."""
    pass
```

**Evidence**: `.opencode/skills/sk-doc/scripts/package_skill.py:87-93`

### Common Type Patterns

```python
from typing import Tuple, List, Dict, Optional, Any, Union
from pathlib import Path

520: ```typescript
521: // Catching typed errors
522: try {
523:   await searchMemories(query, options);
524: } catch (error: unknown) {
525:   if (error instanceof MemoryError) {
526:     // TypeScript narrows to MemoryError — .code and .context available
527:     console.error(`[search] ${error.code}: ${error.message}`);
528:     if (error.code === ErrorCode.Timeout) {
529:       // Handle timeout specifically
530:     }
531:   } else if (error instanceof Error) {
532:     console.error(`[search] Unexpected error: ${error.message}`);
533:   } else {
534:     console.error('[search] Unknown error:', error);
535:   }
536: }
537: ```
538: 
539: **Rule**: Always type `catch` parameter as `unknown` and narrow with `instanceof`.
540: 
541: ---
542: 
543: ## 9. ASYNC PATTERNS
544: 
545: ### Typed Promises
546: 
547: ```typescript
548: // Explicit return type for async functions
549: async function fetchMemories(
550:   query: string,
551: ): Promise<MemoryRecord[]> {
552:   const results = await database.search(query);
553:   return results;
554: }
555: ```
556: 
557: ### Parallel Operations
558: 
559: ```typescript
560: // Parallel execution with typed results
561: const [users, posts]: [User[], Post[]] = await Promise.all([
562:   fetchUsers(),
563:   fetchPosts(),
564: ]);
565: 
566: // Promise.allSettled for partial failure tolerance
567: const results = await Promise.allSettled([
568:   embedText(text1),
569:   embedText(text2),
570:   embedText(text3),
571: ]);
572: 
573: for (const result of results) {
574:   if (result.status === 'fulfilled') {
575:     processEmbedding(result.value);
576:   } else {
577:     console.error(`Embedding failed: ${result.reason}`);
578:   }
579: }
580: ```
581: 
582: ### Async Error Wrapper
583: 
584: ```typescript
585: // Generic async error wrapper with typed result
586: async function safeAsync<T>(
587:   fn: () => Promise<T>,
588:   context: string,
589: ): Promise<OperationResult<T>> {
590:   try {
591:     const data = await fn();
592:     return { success: true, data };
593:   } catch (error: unknown) {
594:     const message = error instanceof Error
595:       ? error.message
596:       : String(error);
597:     console.error(`[${context}] Failed: ${message}`);
598:     return { success: false, error: message, code: ErrorCode.Timeout };
599:   }
600: }
601: 
602: // Usage
603: const result = await safeAsync(
604:   () => searchMemories(query),
605:   'memory-search',
606: );
607: ```
608: 
609: ---
610: 
611: ## 10. TSCONFIG BASELINE
612: 
613: ### Current system-spec-kit package baseline
614: 
615: Current system-spec-kit TypeScript is package-aware, not a single global
616: CommonJS project:
617: 
618: - Root `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/tsconfig.json`: shared defaults,
619:   `module: "commonjs"`, `moduleResolution: "node"`, `strict: true`, project
620:   references.
621: - `shared/`: package `"type": "module"` with `module: "nodenext"` and
622:   `moduleResolution: "nodenext"`.
623: - `mcp_server/`: package `"type": "module"` with `module: "nodenext"`,
624:   `moduleResolution: "nodenext"`, `verbatimModuleSyntax: true`, and `allowJs:
625:   true`.
626: - `scripts/`: package `"type": "module"` with `module: "es2022"` and
627:   `moduleResolution: "node"`.
628: 
629: Use NodeNext ESM guidance for `shared/` and `mcp_server/` source. Use the
630: `scripts/` ESM settings for CLI/memory tooling. Treat the root CommonJS setting
631: as a fallback default for workspaces that do not override it, not as the current
632: MCP server package rule.
633: 
634: ### Root tsconfig.json for OpenCode Projects
635: 
636: ```jsonc
637: {
638:   "compilerOptions": {
639:     // Language and Output
640:     "target": "es2022",
641:     "module": "commonjs",
642:     "moduleResolution": "node",
643: 
644:     // Strict Type Checking (replaces 'use strict')
645:     "strict": true,
646: 
647:     // Interop
648:     "esModuleInterop": true,
649:     "forceConsistentCasingInFileNames": true,
650:     "resolveJsonModule": true,
651:     "skipLibCheck": true,
652: 
653:     // Declarations and Source Maps
654:     "declaration": true,
655:     "declarationMap": true,
656:     "sourceMap": true,
657: 
658:     // Output
659:     "outDir": "./dist",
660:     "rootDir": ".",
661: 
662:     // Project References
663:     "composite": true
664:   },
665:   "references": [
666:     { "path": "./shared" },
667:     { "path": "./mcp_server" },
668:     { "path": "./scripts" }
669:   ],
670:   "exclude": ["node_modules"]
671: }
672: ```
673: 
674: ### Build & Rebuild Workflow
675: 
676: After editing TypeScript source files, the compiled `dist/` output must be rebuilt. The MCP server and CLI scripts run from `dist/`, not from `.ts` source directly.
677: 
678: **Build command** (from skill root, e.g. `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/`):
679: 
680: ```bash
681: # Standard build (type checks + emit)
682: npm run build          # runs: tsc --build
683: 
684: # Build with pre-existing type errors (emit without type checking)
685: npx tsc --build --noCheck --force
686: 
687: # Smoke test after build
688: npm run test:cli       # verifies dist/ output runs correctly
689: ```
690: 
691: **When to use `--noCheck`**: The `strict: true` config combined with third-party SDK types (e.g., MCP SDK `Record<string, unknown>` vs typed handler args) can produce pre-existing type errors that don't affect runtime. Use `--noCheck --force` to emit JavaScript while bypassing these.
692: 
693: **Workspace build order**: `tsc --build` respects project references and builds in dependency order:
694: 1. `shared/` (no dependencies)
695: 2. `mcp_server/` (depends on `shared/`)
696: 3. `scripts/` (depends on `shared/`)
697: 
698: **Post-build verification**:
699: 
700: | Check | Command | Pass Criteria |
701: |-------|---------|---------------|
702: | CLI smoke test | `npm run test:cli` | Exit 0 + "CLI smoke test passed" |
703: | MCP server start | `npm run start` | Server starts without crash |
704: | Embedding test | `npm run test:embeddings` | Embeddings factory loads |
705: 
706: **Common TS4094 error**: If exporting a class with private/protected members from a `.js` file re-exported through `.ts`, TypeScript cannot emit declarations. Fix by adding a type assertion at the re-export site:
707: 
708: ```typescript
709: // TS4094: Property '_method' of exported anonymous class type may not be private
710: // Fix: widen the type to suppress declaration emit error
711: const ExportedClass = jsModule.SomeClass as typeof jsModule.SomeClass & { new(...args: unknown[]): unknown };
712: ```
713: 
714: ### Workspace override decisions
715: 
716: | Workspace | Package Type | Module Settings | Rationale |
717: |-----------|--------------|-----------------|-----------|
718: | `shared/` | `"type": "module"` | `module: "nodenext"`, `moduleResolution: "nodenext"` | Shared runtime library consumed as ESM |
719: | `mcp_server/` | `"type": "module"` | `module: "nodenext"`, `moduleResolution: "nodenext"`, `verbatimModuleSyntax: true` | Current MCP server package and API exports are ESM |
720: | `scripts/` | `"type": "module"` | `module: "es2022"`, `moduleResolution: "node"` | CLI/memory scripts run as ESM and compile to `dist/` |
721: 
722: ### Root fallback configuration decisions
723: 
724: | Setting                           | Value       | Rationale                                                |
725: |-----------------------------------|-------------|----------------------------------------------------------|
726: | `target`                          | `es2022`    | Node.js 18+ supports ES2022 features natively            |
727: | `module`                          | `commonjs`  | Root fallback only; active workspaces override where needed |
728: | `strict`                          | `true`      | Full strict mode from the start (Decision D3)            |
729: | `composite`                       | `true`      | Enables project references for incremental builds        |
730: | `outDir`                          | `"./dist"`  | Compiled output in dist/ folder, matches all 3 workspace tsconfigs |
731: | `rootDir`                         | `"."`       | Source root at project level                                  |
732: | `esModuleInterop`                 | `true`      | Allows interop where CommonJS dependencies are consumed  |
733: | `declaration`                     | `true`      | Generates `.d.ts` files for cross-project type checking  |
734: | `skipLibCheck`                    | `true`      | Faster builds; third-party `.d.ts` issues ignored        |
735: 
736: ### Workspace tsconfig.json Pattern
737: 
738: Each workspace extends the root and declares its own composite settings:
739: 
740: ```jsonc
741: // shared/tsconfig.json
742: {
743:   "extends": "../tsconfig.json",
744:   "compilerOptions": {
745:     "composite": true,
746:     "rootDir": ".",
747:     "outDir": "./dist"
748:   },
749:   "include": ["**/*.ts"],
750:   "exclude": ["node_modules"]
751: }
752: 
753: // mcp_server/tsconfig.json (references shared/)
754: {
755:   "extends": "../tsconfig.json",
756:   "compilerOptions": {
757:     "composite": true,
758:     "rootDir": ".",
759:     "outDir": "./dist"
760:   },
761:   "references": [
762:     { "path": "../shared" }
763:   ],
764:   "include": ["**/*.ts"],
765:   "exclude": ["node_modules"]
766: }
767: ```
768: 
769: ---
770: 
771: ## 11. MODULE ORGANIZATION
772: 
773: ### ES Module Source, Package-Aware Output
774: 
775: TypeScript source files use ES module syntax (`import`/`export`). The emitted
776: module format follows the workspace `tsconfig.json` and `package.json` boundary:
777: NodeNext ESM for `shared/` and `mcp_server/`, ES2022 ESM for `scripts/`, and
778: CommonJS only where a workspace inherits the root fallback.
779: 

(Showing lines 520-779 of 824. Use offset=780 to continue.)
</content>

269:  */
270: ```
271: 
272: ### Class Documentation
273: 
274: ```javascript
275: /**
276:  * Manages vector-based semantic search index.
277:  *
278:  * @class
279:  * @example
280:  * const index = new VectorIndex(dbPath);
281:  * await index.initialize();
282:  */
283: class VectorIndex {
284:   /**
285:    * Create a VectorIndex instance.
286:    * @param {string} dbPath - Path to SQLite database
287:    */
288:   constructor(dbPath) {
289:     this.dbPath = dbPath;
290:   }
291: }
292: ```
293: 
294: ---
295: 
296: ## 6. SECURITY PATTERNS
297: 
298: ### CWE-22: Path Traversal Prevention
299: 
300: Validate and sanitize file paths.
301: 
302: ```javascript
303: const path = require('path');
304: 
305: /**
306:  * Safely resolve path within allowed directory.
307:  * @param {string} basePath - Allowed base directory
308:  * @param {string} userPath - User-provided path
309:  * @returns {string|null} Resolved path or null if invalid
310:  */
311: function safeResolvePath(basePath, userPath) {
312:   // Normalize and resolve
313:   const resolvedBase = path.resolve(basePath);
314:   const resolvedPath = path.resolve(basePath, userPath);
315: 
316:   // Verify path stays within base
317:   if (!resolvedPath.startsWith(resolvedBase + path.sep)) {
318:     console.error(`[security] Path traversal attempt: ${userPath}`);
319:     return null;
320:   }
321: 
322:   return resolvedPath;
323: }
324: ```
325: 
326: ### CWE-400: Input Limits
327: 
328: Prevent resource exhaustion with input validation.
329: 
330: ```javascript
331: const MAX_QUERY_LENGTH = 10000;
332: const MAX_RESULTS = 100;
333: 
334: function validateSearchInput(query, options) {
335:   // Limit query length
336:   if (query && query.length > MAX_QUERY_LENGTH) {
337:     return { valid: false, error: `Query exceeds ${MAX_QUERY_LENGTH} characters` };
338:   }
339: 
340:   // Limit result count
341:   if (options.limit && options.limit > MAX_RESULTS) {
342:     options.limit = MAX_RESULTS;
343:     console.warn(`[search] Limit capped to ${MAX_RESULTS}`);
344:   }
345: 
346:   return { valid: true };
347: }
348: ```
349: 
350: ### Input Sanitization
351: 
352: ```javascript
353: /**
354:  * Sanitize string input for safe use.
355:  * @param {string} input - Raw user input
356:  * @returns {string} Sanitized string
357:  */
358: function sanitizeString(input) {
359:   if (typeof input !== 'string') {
360:     return '';
361:   }
362: 
363:   // Remove null bytes
364:   return input.replace(/\0/g, '');
365: }
366: ```
367: 
368: ---
369: 
370: ## 7. TESTING PATTERNS
371: 
372: ### Using Node.js Assert
373: 
374: ```javascript
375: const assert = require('assert');
376: 
377: // Test function
378: function testLoadConfig() {
379:   const config = loadConfig('./test-config.yaml');
380: 
381:   assert.ok(config, 'Config should be loaded');
382:   assert.strictEqual(config.version, '1.0.0', 'Version should match');
383:   assert.deepStrictEqual(config.features, ['a', 'b'], 'Features should match');
384: }
385: 
386: // Run tests
387: if (require.main === module) {
388:   testLoadConfig();
389:   console.log('[test] All tests passed');
390: }
391: ```
392: 
393: ### Test File Organization
394: 
395: ```javascript
396: // tests/test_config.js
397: 'use strict';
398: 
399: const assert = require('assert');
400: const { loadConfig, validateConfig } = require('../scripts/core/config');
401: 
402: describe('Config Module', () => {
403:   describe('loadConfig', () => {
404:     it('should load valid YAML config', () => {
405:       const config = loadConfig('./fixtures/valid.yaml');
406:       assert.ok(config);
407:     });
408: 
409:     it('should return null for missing file', () => {
410:       const config = loadConfig('./nonexistent.yaml');
411:       assert.strictEqual(config, null);
412:     });
413:   });
414: });
415: ```
416: 
417: ---
418: 
419: ## 8. ASYNC PATTERNS
420: 
421: ### Async/Await Style
422: 
423: Prefer async/await over callbacks and raw promises.
424: 
425: ```javascript
426: // CORRECT: async/await
427: async function fetchAndProcess() {
428:   try {
429:     const data = await fetchData();
430:     const result = await processData(data);
431:     return { success: true, data: result };
432:   } catch (error) {
433:     return { success: false, error: error.message };
434:   }
435: }
436: 
437: // AVOID: Promise chains
438: function fetchAndProcess() {
439:   return fetchData()
440:     .then(data => processData(data))
441:     .then(result => ({ success: true, data: result }))
442:     .catch(error => ({ success: false, error: error.message }));
443: }
444: ```
445: 
446: ### Parallel Operations
447: 
448: ```javascript
449: // Parallel execution
450: const [users, posts] = await Promise.all([
451:   fetchUsers(),
452:   fetchPosts()
453: ]);
454: 
455: // Sequential execution (when order matters)
456: const user = await fetchUser(id);
457: const posts = await fetchUserPosts(user.id);
458: ```
459: 
460: ---
461: 
462: ## 9. TEST FILE EXEMPTION TIER
463: 
464: CLI-only test runners, setup scripts, and similar utilities are exempt from certain quality standards because they are executed directly (not imported as modules) and serve a different purpose than library code.
465: 
466: ### Scope
467: 
468: This exemption applies to:
469: - Files in `scripts/tests/`
470: - Files in `scripts/setup/`
471: - Any file with a test runner shebang (e.g., `#!/usr/bin/env node`)
472: 
473: ### Exempted Standards
474: 
475: | Standard                           | Reason for Exemption                                           |
476: |------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------|
477: | `module.exports` requirement       | Test runners are executed directly, not imported by other code |
478: | Guard clause requirement           | Scripts run once at CLI level; input validation is minimal     |
479: | `[module-name]` error prefix       | Test output uses its own format (e.g., pass/fail assertions)   |
480: 
481: ### What Still Applies
482: 
483: All other quality standards remain in effect for test files, including:
484: - `'use strict'` directive
485: - JSDoc documentation for non-trivial functions
486: - Error handling with try-catch for async operations
487: - Security patterns (path traversal, input limits) if handling external input
488: 
489: ### Example
490: 
491: ```javascript
492: #!/usr/bin/env node
493: 'use strict';
494: 
495: const assert = require('assert');
496: const { loadConfig } = require('../core/config');
497: 
498: // No module.exports needed — this file is executed directly
499: // No guard clauses needed — CLI-level script
500: // No [module-name] prefix needed — test output format
501: 
502: const config = loadConfig('./fixtures/valid.yaml');
503: assert.ok(config, 'Config should load successfully');
504: assert.strictEqual(config.version, '1.0.0');
505: 
506: console.log('PASS: All config tests passed');
507: ```
508: 
509: ---
510: 
511: ## 10. OPENCODE PLUGIN EXEMPTION TIER
512: 
513: OpenCode plugin entrypoints and helper modules are exempt from the CommonJS export standard because the OpenCode plugin loader requires ESM default exports. This exemption is narrow and does not relax other JavaScript quality standards.
514: 
515: ### Scope
516: 
517: This exemption applies to:
518: - `.opencode/plugins/*.{js,mjs,ts}`
519: - `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/plugin_bridges/*.{js,mjs,ts}`
520:

</content>

1: ---
2: title: Python Quality Standards
3: description: Code quality requirements, validation rules, and best practices for Python scripts in the OpenCode development environment.
4: trigger_phrases:
5:   - "opencode python quality standards"
6:   - "python quality gates"
7:   - "python module docstring rules"
8:   - "snake case function naming"
9: importance_tier: normal
10: contextType: implementation
11: ---
12: 
13: # Python Quality Standards
14: 
15: Code quality requirements, validation rules, and best practices for Python scripts in the OpenCode development environment.
16: 
17: ---
18: 
19: ## 1. OVERVIEW
20: 
21: ### Purpose
22: 
23: Establishes quality gates and validation criteria that all Python code must meet before being committed or deployed.
24: 
25: ### Quality Tiers
26: 
27: | Tier | Requirement Level | Enforcement |
28: |------|-------------------|-------------|
29: | P0 | HARD BLOCKER | Must fix before commit |
30: | P1 | Required | Must fix or get approval |
31: | P2 | Recommended | Can defer with justification |
32: 
33: ---
34: 
35: ## 2. P0 - HARD BLOCKERS
36: 
37: These items MUST be fixed before any commit.
38: 
39: ### File Header Present
40: 
41: Every Python script must have the standard header:
42: 
43: ```python
44: #!/usr/bin/env python3
45: # ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
46: # COMPONENT: [COMPONENT NAME]
47: # ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
48: ```
49: 
50: **Evidence**: `.opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/scripts/skill_advisor.py:1-4`
51: 
52: ### Module Docstring
53: 
54: Every script must have a module-level docstring explaining purpose and usage:
55: 
56: ```python
57: """
58: Brief description of what this module does.
59: 
60: Usage: python script_name.py [arguments]
61: """
62: ```
63: 
64: **Evidence**: `.opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/scripts/skill_advisor.py:6-16`
65: 
66: ### snake_case Functions
67: 
68: All function names must use snake_case:
69: 
70: ```python
71: # Correct
72: def validate_frontmatter(content: str) -> bool:
73:     pass
74: 
75: # WRONG - will fail validation
76: def validateFrontmatter(content: str) -> bool:  # camelCase
77:     pass
78: 
79: def ValidateFrontmatter(content: str) -> bool:  # PascalCase
80:     pass
81: ```
82: 
83: **Evidence**: `.opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/scripts/skill_advisor.py`, `.opencode/skills/sk-doc/scripts/package_skill.py:87`
84: 
85: ### UPPER_SNAKE_CASE Constants
86: 
87: Module-level constants must use UPPER_SNAKE_CASE:
88: 
89: ```python
90: # Correct
91: MAX_FILE_SIZE = 5000
92: REQUIRED_FIELDS = ['name', 'description']
93: 
94: # WRONG
95: maxFileSize = 5000  # camelCase
96: required_fields = ['name', 'description']  # snake_case (looks like variable)
97: ```
98: 
99: **Evidence**: `.opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/scripts/skill_advisor.py:31-49`
100: 
101: ### No Commented-Out Code
102: 
103: Remove all commented-out code blocks. Use version control for history.
104: 
105: ```python
106: # WRONG - remove this
107: # def old_function():
108: #     return "deprecated"
109: 
110: # OK - explanatory comments
111: # Note: This approach chosen because...
112: ```
113: 
114: ### WHY Comments for Non-Obvious Code
115: 
116: Complex logic must include comments explaining WHY, not WHAT:
117: 
118: ```python
119: # WRONG - describes what (obvious from code)
120: # Set x to 5
121: x = 5
122: 
123: # CORRECT - explains why
124: # Use 5 retries to handle transient network failures (per SLA requirement)
125: max_retries = 5
126: ```
127: 
128: ---
129: 
130: ## 3. P1 - REQUIRED
131: 
132: These items must be addressed or receive explicit approval to defer.
133: 
134: ### Type Hints
135: 
136: All function signatures should include type hints:
137: 
138: ```python
139: # Required for all functions
140: def process_data(
141:     input_data: Dict[str, Any],
142:     strict: bool = True
143: ) -> Tuple[bool, str, List[str]]:
144:     pass
145: ```
146: 
147: **Evidence**: `.opencode/skills/sk-doc/scripts/package_skill.py:87`
148: 
149: ### Google-Style Docstrings
150: 
151: Public functions must have Google-style docstrings:
152: 
153: ```python
154: def validate_config(config_path: Path) -> Tuple[bool, str]:
155:     """
156:     Validate configuration file against schema.
157: 
158:     Args:
159:         config_path: Path to the configuration file
160: 
161:     Returns:
162:         Tuple of (is_valid, error_message)
163: 
164:     Raises:
165:         FileNotFoundError: If config_path doesn't exist
166:     """
167:     pass
168: ```
169: 
170: **Evidence**: `.opencode/skills/sk-doc/scripts/package_skill.py:87-93`
171: 
172: ### Specific Exception Handling
173: 
174: Catch specific exceptions, never bare `except`:
175: 
176: ```python
177: # Required
178: try:
179:     data = json.loads(content)
180: except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
181:     logger.error(f"JSON parse error: {e}")
182:     return None
183: 
184: # WRONG - too broad
185: try:
186:     data = json.loads(content)
187: except:
188:     return None
189: ```
190: 
191: ### Early Return Pattern
192: 
193: Use guard clauses and early returns to reduce nesting:
194: 
195: ```python
196: # Required pattern
197: def process(data):
198:     if not data:
199:         return None
200: 
201:     if not validate(data):
202:         return None
203: 
204:     # Main logic at base level
205:     return transform(data)
206: 
207: # WRONG - deep nesting
208: def process(data):
209:     if data:
210:         if validate(data):
211:             return transform(data)
212:     return None
213: ```
214: 
215: **Evidence**: `.opencode/skills/sk-doc/scripts/package_skill.py:98-104`
216: 
217: ### TODO Format
218: 
219: TODOs must include context (ticket number or owner):
220: 
221: ```python
222: # Required format
223: # TODO(username): Description of what needs to be done
224: # TODO(TICKET-123): Implement caching for performance
225: 
226: # WRONG - no context
227: # TODO: fix this later
228: ```
229: 
230: ---
231: 
232: ## 4. P2 - RECOMMENDED
233: 
234: These items improve quality but can be deferred with justification.
235: 
236: ### Import Ordering
237: 
238: Follow PEP 8 import order with blank lines between groups:
239: 
240: ```python
241: # 1. Standard library
242: import os
243: import sys
244: from pathlib import Path
245: 
246: # 2. Third-party
247: import yaml
248: 
249: # 3. Local
250: from .utils import helper
251: ```
252: 
253: ### Consistent String Quotes
254: 
255: Use double quotes for strings consistently:
256: 
257: ```python
258: # Preferred
259: message = "Hello world"
260: config = {"key": "value"}
261: 
262: # Acceptable but less preferred
263: message = 'Hello world'
264: ```
265: 
266: ### Line Length
267: 
268: - Target: 88-100 characters
269: - Maximum: 120 characters
270: - Break at logical points for readability
271: 
272: ### Docstring Coverage
273: 
274: Aim for docstrings on all public functions and classes, even simple ones:
275: 
276: ```python
277: def get_version() -> str:
278:     """Return the current version string."""
279:     return VERSION
280: ```
281: 
282: ---
283: 
284: ## 5. VALIDATION PATTERNS
285: 
286: ### Function Validation Pattern
287: 
288: Standard pattern for functions that validate input:
289: 
290: ```python
291: def validate_something(input_data: Any) -> Tuple[bool, str, List[str]]:
292:     """
293:     Validate input against requirements.
294: 
295:     Args:
296:         input_data: The data to validate
297: 
298:     Returns:
299:         Tuple of (is_valid, error_message, warnings)
300:     """

(Showing lines 1-300 of 450. Use offset=301 to continue.)
</content>

# Python Quality Standards

Code quality requirements, validation rules, and best practices for Python scripts in the OpenCode development environment.

---

## 1. OVERVIEW

### Purpose

Establishes quality gates and validation criteria that all Python code must meet before being committed or deployed.

### Quality Tiers

| Tier | Requirement Level | Enforcement |
|------|-------------------|-------------|
| P0 | HARD BLOCKER | Must fix before commit |
| P1 | Required | Must fix or get approval |
| P2 | Recommended | Can defer with justification |

---

## 2. P0 - HARD BLOCKERS

These items MUST be fixed before any commit.

### File Header Present

Every Python script must have the standard header:

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# COMPONENT: [COMPONENT NAME]
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
```

**Evidence**: `.opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/scripts/skill_advisor.py:1-4`

### Module Docstring

Every script must have a module-level docstring explaining purpose and usage:

```python
"""
Brief description of what this module does.

Usage: python script_name.py [arguments]
"""
```

**Evidence**: `.opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/scripts/skill_advisor.py:6-16`

### snake_case Functions

All function names must use snake_case:

```python
# Correct
def validate_frontmatter(content: str) -> bool:
    pass

# WRONG - will fail validation
def validateFrontmatter(content: str) -> bool:  # camelCase
    pass

def ValidateFrontmatter(content: str) -> bool:  # PascalCase
    pass
```

**Evidence**: `.opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/scripts/skill_advisor.py`, `.opencode/skills/sk-doc/scripts/package_skill.py:87`

### UPPER_SNAKE_CASE Constants

Module-level constants must use UPPER_SNAKE_CASE:

```python
# Correct
MAX_FILE_SIZE = 5000
REQUIRED_FIELDS = ['name', 'description']

# WRONG
maxFileSize = 5000  # camelCase
required_fields = ['name', 'description']  # snake_case (looks like variable)
```

**Evidence**: `.opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/scripts/skill_advisor.py:31-49`

### No Commented-Out Code

Remove all commented-out code blocks. Use version control for history.

```python
# WRONG - remove this
# def old_function():
#     return "deprecated"

# OK - explanatory comments
# Note: This approach chosen because...
```

### WHY Comments for Non-Obvious Code

Complex logic must include comments explaining WHY, not WHAT:

```python
# WRONG - describes what (obvious from code)
# Set x to 5
x = 5

# CORRECT - explains why
# Use 5 retries to handle transient network failures (per SLA requirement)
max_retries = 5
```

---

## 3. P1 - REQUIRED

These items must be addressed or receive explicit approval to defer.

### Type Hints

All function signatures should include type hints:

```python
# Required for all functions
def process_data(
    input_data: Dict[str, Any],
    strict: bool = True
) -> Tuple[bool, str, List[str]]:
    pass
```

**Evidence**: `.opencode/skills/sk-doc/scripts/package_skill.py:87`

### Google-Style Docstrings

Public functions must have Google-style docstrings:

```python
def validate_config(config_path: Path) -> Tuple[bool, str]:
    """
    Validate configuration file against schema.

    Args:
        config_path: Path to the configuration file

    Returns:
        Tuple of (is_valid, error_message)

    Raises:
        FileNotFoundError: If config_path doesn't exist
    """
    pass
```

**Evidence**: `.opencode/skills/sk-doc/scripts/package_skill.py:87-93`

### Specific Exception Handling

Catch specific exceptions, never bare `except`:

```python
# Required
try:
    data = json.loads(content)
except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
    logger.error(f"JSON parse error: {e}")
    return None

# WRONG - too broad
try:
    data = json.loads(content)
except:
    return None
```

### Early Return Pattern

Use guard clauses and early returns to reduce nesting:

```python
# Required pattern
def process(data):
    if not data:
        return None

    if not validate(data):
        return None

    # Main logic at base level
    return transform(data)

# WRONG - deep nesting
def process(data):
    if data:
        if validate(data):
            return transform(data)
    return None
```

**Evidence**: `.opencode/skills/sk-doc/scripts/package_skill.py:98-104`

### TODO Format

TODOs must include context (ticket number or owner):

```python
# Required format
# TODO(username): Description of what needs to be done
# TODO(TICKET-123): Implement caching for performance

# WRONG - no context
# TODO: fix this later
```

---

## 4. P2 - RECOMMENDED

These items improve quality but can be deferred with justification.

### Import Ordering

Follow PEP 8 import order with blank lines between groups:

```python
# 1. Standard library
import os
import sys
from pathlib import Path

# 2. Third-party
import yaml

# 3. Local
from .utils import helper
```

### Consistent String Quotes

Use double quotes for strings consistently:

```python
# Preferred
message = "Hello world"
config = {"key": "value"}

# Acceptable but less preferred
message = 'Hello world'
```

### Line Length

- Target: 88-100 characters
- Maximum: 120 characters
- Break at logical points for readability

### Docstring Coverage

Aim for docstrings on all public functions and classes, even simple ones:

```python
def get_version() -> str:
    """Return the current version string."""
    return VERSION
```

---

## 5. VALIDATION PATTERNS

### Function Validation Pattern

Standard pattern for functions that validate input:

```python
def validate_something(input_data: Any) -> Tuple[bool, str, List[str]]:
    """
    Validate input against requirements.

    Args:
        input_data: The data to validate

    Returns:
        Tuple of (is_valid, error_message, warnings)
    """

# MCP Server Authoring Checklist

Checklist for authoring or modifying OpenCode MCP servers with correct tool registration, schema validation, typed responses, and consistent error envelopes.

## 1. OVERVIEW

### Purpose

This checklist covers the foundation needed for a new OpenCode MCP server to be discoverable, typed, and operationally safe. It helps authors choose Python versus TypeScript patterns, register tools correctly, and keep response/error behavior consistent with existing servers.

### Usage

- Use this when authoring a new MCP server under `.opencode/skills/<skill-name>/mcp_server/`.
- Use this when adding or changing MCP tools, input schemas, registry entries, or response envelopes.
- Use this when porting a shell or CLI workflow into an MCP-accessible service.
- Use this when changing Python or TypeScript MCP server startup behavior.

---

## 2. PRE-CHECKS

- [ ] Read canonical MCP server examples at `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/`, `.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/`, and `.opencode/skills/sk-prompt/mcp_server/` when present.
- [ ] Decide whether the server should be Python or TypeScript based on existing skill runtime, dependencies, and typed schema needs.
- [ ] Verify MCP tool registration in the relevant registry or server entrypoint.
- [ ] Define input schema validation before implementing handler logic.
- [ ] Confirm response shape uses the local success/error envelope convention.
- [ ] Plan tests for schema rejection, happy path, and handler error behavior.

---

## 3. STEPS

1. Choose the server language and entrypoint that matches the owning skill.
2. Add tool schemas before handler implementation.
3. Register each tool in the MCP server registry or dispatch table.
4. Implement handlers with explicit validation, bounded filesystem access, and structured responses.
5. Use the shared error envelope or response helpers used by the nearest canonical server.
6. Add tests or fixture checks that cover malformed input, successful output, and thrown errors.
7. Document install or runtime requirements in the owning skill when users must configure the server.

---

## 4. POST-CHECKS

- [ ] Run `bash .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/spec/validate.sh <path> --strict` when the MCP server change is part of a spec folder.
- [ ] grep verification: `rg -n "schema|register|tool|error|envelope|success" .opencode/skills/<skill-name>/mcp_server`.
- [ ] Run the targeted Python, TypeScript, or package test command used by the owning MCP server.
- [ ] Cross-runtime mirror parity check if an agent, command, or skill mirror advertises the new MCP capability.

---

## 5. RELATED RESOURCES

- sk-doc references/skill_creation.md (source-of-truth for skill packaging around MCP assets)
- Prior examples: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/`, `.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/`, `.opencode/skills/sk-prompt/mcp_server/`
- Verification recipes: `.opencode/skills/sk-code/assets/opencode/checklists/typescript_checklist.md`, `.opencode/skills/sk-code/assets/opencode/checklists/python_checklist.md`

12: 
13: # Universal Code Quality Checklist
14: 
15: Language-agnostic quality checks that apply to all code files in the OpenCode development environment.
16: 
17: ---
18: 
19: ## 1. OVERVIEW
20: 
21: ### Purpose
22: 
23: Provides baseline quality requirements that apply across all languages (JavaScript, TypeScript, Python, Shell, Config). Use this checklist alongside language-specific checklists.
24: 
25: ### Priority Levels
26: 
27: | Level | Meaning | Enforcement |
28: |-------|---------|-------------|
29: | P0 | HARD BLOCKER | Must fix before commit |
30: | P1 | Required | Must fix or get explicit approval |
31: | P2 | Recommended | Can defer with justification |
32: 
33: ---
34: 
35: ## 2. P0 - HARD BLOCKERS
36: 
37: These items MUST be fixed before any commit.
38: 
39: ### File Header
40: 
41: ```markdown
42: [ ] File has appropriate header comment identifying the component
43:     - JavaScript: Box header with 'use strict'
44:     - TypeScript: Module header block `// ─── MODULE: NAME ───` (no 'use strict'; tsconfig handles it)
45:     - Python: Shebang + COMPONENT comment block
46:     - Shell: Shebang + COMPONENT comment block
47:     - Config: JSONC comment header
48: ```
49: 
50: **Evidence**: All style guides require headers
51: 
52: **Verifier boundary**: `verify_alignment_drift.py` checks marker-level headers
53: for supported code/config files. Exact visual style remains a manual checklist
54: gate.
55: 
56: ### No Commented-Out Code
57: 
58: ```markdown
59: [ ] No commented-out code blocks present
60:     - Use version control for code history
61:     - Explanatory comments are OK
62:     - TODO comments with context are OK
63: ```
64: 
65: **Verifier boundary**: Commented-out code and WHY-vs-WHAT comment quality are
66: manual review gates; the alignment verifier does not parse intent from comments.
67: 
68: **What to remove**:
69: ```javascript
70: // REMOVE - commented code
71: // function oldImplementation() {
72: //     return "deprecated";
73: // }
74: 
75: // KEEP - explanatory comment
76: // Note: Using setTimeout because requestAnimationFrame not available in Node
77: ```
78: 
79: ### No Ephemeral-Artifact Pointers in Comments
80: 
81: ```markdown
82: [ ] No spec/packet/phase numbers, T###/REQ-###/CHK-###, feature-catalog, ADR, or ticket ids in comments
83:     - Keep the durable WHY; drop the perishable label
84:     - Structural path/globs the code needs (e.g. .opencode/specs/) and stable standards (CWE-###) are OK
85:     - See references/universal/code_style_guide.md §4 "No ephemeral-artifact pointers"
86: ```
87: 
88: ### Numbered ALL-CAPS Section Invariant
89: 
90: ```markdown
91: [ ] Numbered ALL-CAPS section headers are preserved
92:     - Use `## N. SECTION NAME` in standards/examples
93:     - Do not switch to sentence-case section names
94: ```
95: 
96: ### Filesystem Mutation Safety
97: 
98: ```markdown
99: [ ] File and folder mutations are guarded by canonical path checks
100:     - Use realpath/canonical path resolution for candidate and allowed roots
101:     - Use relative/boundary-safe containment checks (not substring-only checks)
102:     - Reject traversal/symlink escape attempts before create/move/delete
103: ```
104: 
105: ### Spec Folder Invariants (when touching specs)
106: 
107: ```markdown
108: [ ] Spec folder operations enforce `NNN-name` and approved roots
109:     - Allowed roots: `specs/` and `.opencode/specs/` only
110:     - Archive/restore/create targets validated before execution
111:     - Do not emit restore instructions unless checkpoint creation succeeded
112: ```
113: 
114: ---
115: 
116: ## 3. P1 - REQUIRED
117: 
118: These must be addressed or receive approval to defer.
119: 
120: ### Consistent Naming
121: 
122: ```markdown
123: [ ] Naming follows language conventions
124:     - JavaScript: camelCase functions, UPPER_SNAKE constants
125:     - TypeScript: camelCase functions, PascalCase interfaces/types/enums, UPPER_SNAKE constants
126:     - Python: snake_case functions, UPPER_SNAKE constants
127:     - Shell: snake_case functions, UPPER_SNAKE constants
128:     - Config: camelCase keys
129: ```
130: 
131: ### WHY Comments (Manual Review)
132: 
133: ```markdown
134: [ ] Purposeful WHY comments only (P1, manual checklist gate)
135:     - Comments explain WHY, not WHAT
136:     - Maximum 3 comments per 10 LOC
137:     - No narrative comments describing obvious mechanics
138: ```
139: 
140: **Bad (WHAT)**:
141: ```python
142: # Set x to 5
143: x = 5
144: ```
145: 
146: **Good (WHY)**:
147: ```python
148: # use 5 retries per SLA requirement for transient network failures
149: max_retries = 5
150: ```
151: 
152: ### TODO Format
153: 
154: ```markdown
155: [ ] All TODOs include context (owner or ticket number)
156: ```
157: 
158: **Bad**:
159: ```javascript
160: // TODO: fix this later
161: ```
162: 
163: **Good**:
164: ```javascript
165: // TODO(username): Add input validation
166: // TODO(TICKET-123): Handle edge case for empty arrays
167: ```
168: 
169: ### Error Messages
170: 
171: ```markdown
172: [ ] Error messages include context
173:     - What failed
174:     - Why it failed (if known)
175:     - How to fix (if applicable)
176: ```
177: 
178: **Bad**:
179: ```python
180: return False, "Invalid input"
181: ```
182: 
183: **Good**:
184: ```python
185: return False, f"Name '{name}' must be hyphen-case (lowercase letters, digits, and hyphens only)"
186: ```
187: 
188: ### No Magic Numbers/Strings
189: 
190: ```markdown
191: [ ] Magic values are extracted to named constants
192: ```
193: 
194: **Bad**:
195: ```javascript
196: if (items.length > 100) {  // What is 100?
197: ```
198: 
199: **Good**:
200: ```javascript
201: const MAX_ITEMS = 100;  // API rate limit per request
202: if (items.length > MAX_ITEMS) {
203: ```
204: 
205: ### KISS / DRY / SOLID Gate
206: 
207: ```markdown
208: [ ] KISS/DRY/SOLID checks applied before merge
209:     - KISS: no speculative abstraction layers
210:     - DRY: repeated rules/constants consolidated
211:     - SOLID: SRP/OCP/LSP/ISP/DIP violations reviewed and resolved or documented
212: ```
213: 
214: ---
215: 
216: ## 4. P2 - RECOMMENDED
217: 
218: These improve quality but can be deferred.
219: 
220: ### Comment Density
221: 
222: ```markdown
223: [ ] Appropriate level of documentation
224:     - Public APIs have documentation
225:     - Complex algorithms explained
226:     - Not over-commented (target <= 3 comments per 10 LOC)
227: ```
228: 
229: ### Code Organization
230: 
231: ```markdown
232: [ ] Code is organized into logical sections
233:     - Related functionality grouped
234:     - Clear separation of concerns
235:     - Consistent ordering (constants, functions, exports)
236: ```
237: 
238: ### Line Length
239: 
240: ```markdown
