return bool(
        re.search(
            r"\b(figure out|find|diagnose|debug)\b.{0,40}\b(wrong|broken|failing|bug|issue)\b.{0,40}\bcode\b",
            prompt_lower,
        )
        or re.search(r"\b(wrong|broken|failing)\b.{0,30}\bcode\b", prompt_lower)
    )


def _apply_memory_save_file_operation_cap(
    recommendations: List[Dict[str, Any]],
    prompt_lower: str,
) -> None:
    """Keep plain file-save prompts from passing as memory-save command intent."""
    if not _is_plain_file_save_prompt(prompt_lower):
        return
    for recommendation in recommendations:
        if recommendation.get("skill") in {"memory:save", "command-memory-save"}:
            recommendation["confidence"] = min(float(recommendation.get("confidence", 0.0)), 0.49)


# Candidate-3 deep-skill routing, per 130 research.md §5-§6:
#   total_score = (lexical_score * 1.0) + (structural_score * 2.0) + (prior_art_score * 3.0)
# Fallback to Candidate 2 happens naturally when packet_context is empty: prior_art_score=0.
# LOW confidence (<0.65) returns a clarifying_question payload. This closes 130
# where "iterate findings until convergence" was DANGEROUS because all deep-* loops
# have different convergence and findings semantics.
DEEP_ROUTING_SKILLS = ("deep-review", "deep-research", "deep-ai-council")
DEEP_ROUTING_CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD = 0.65

# The five legacy deep-loop skills are folded into one public skill,
# deep-loop-workflows, discriminated by workflowMode. The Candidate-3 internal
# discriminator keys below stay spelled as the legacy skill ids because the
# regex pattern groups and prior-art matchers key off them and match live
# artifact/agent names still present across the live agent and artifact surfaces;
# DEEP_ROUTING_MODE_BY_KEY projects each onto the merged skill's workflowMode so
# the routing contract emits {skill: deep-loop-workflows, mode}. deep-context is
# intentionally NOT a Candidate-3 discriminator: it stays metadata-routed
# (resolved from its graph-metadata.json), so DEEP_ROUTING_SKILLS stays 3.
MERGED_DEEP_SKILL_ID = "deep-loop-workflows"
DEEP_ROUTING_MODE_BY_KEY = {

<path>/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/scripts/check-skill-doc-frontmatter.mjs</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: #!/usr/bin/env node
2: // ====================================================================
3: // check-skill-doc-frontmatter.mjs — canonical frontmatter contract
4: //                                    checker for skill reference/asset docs
5: // ====================================================================
6: // Dependency-free on purpose: CI and per-phase authoring runs invoke it
7: // at the repo root without an npm install. Parsing mirrors the harvest
8: // semantics in lib/skill-graph/doc-frontmatter.ts (leading fence only,
9: // block + inline list forms, trim before quote-strip).
10: //
11: // Contract (references/assets, README.md exempt):
12: //   title            non-empty
13: //   description      non-empty
14: //   trigger_phrases  3-8 non-empty items
15: //   importance_tier  constitutional|critical|important|normal|temporary|deprecated
16: //   contextType      planning|research|implementation|general
17: //
18: // Modes:
19: //   --shape    (default) a doc carrying ANY detailed field must be fully valid;
20: //              docs with title+description only, or no frontmatter, pass.
21: //   --coverage every reference/asset doc must carry the full valid block.
22: //
23: // Output: one "FAIL <path>: <reason>" line per violation, then a summary.
24: // Exit: 0 when no violations, 1 otherwise.
25: 
26: import { readdirSync, readFileSync } from 'node:fs';
27: import { join, relative, basename } from 'node:path';
28: 
29: const IMPORTANCE_TIERS = new Set([
30:   'constitutional', 'critical', 'important', 'normal', 'temporary', 'deprecated',
31: ]);
32: const CONTEXT_TYPES = new Set(['planning', 'research', 'implementation', 'general']);
33: const DETAILED_FIELDS = ['trigger_phrases', 'importance_tier', 'contextType'];
34: const DOC_SUBDIRS = ['references', 'assets'];
35: 
36: function parseArgs(argv) {
37:   const opts = { root: '.', mode: 'shape', skill: null };
38:   for (let i = 0; i < argv.length; i += 1) {
39:     const arg = argv[i];
40:     if (arg === '--shape') opts.mode = 'shape';
41:     else if (arg === '--coverage') opts.mode = 'coverage';
42:     else if (arg === '--skill') { opts.skill = argv[i + 1] ?? null; i += 1; }
43:     else if (!arg.startsWith('-')) opts.root = arg;
44:     else {
45:       process.stderr.write(`unknown option: ${arg}\n`);
46:       process.exit(2);
47:     }
48:   }
49:   return opts;
50: }
51: 
52: function cleanScalar(raw) {
53:   let value = raw.trim();
54:   if (
55:     value.length >= 2 &&
56:     ((value.startsWith('"') && value.endsWith('"')) ||
57:       (value.startsWith("'") && value.endsWith("'")))
58:   ) {
59:     value = value.slice(1, -1).trim();
60:   }
61:   return value;
62: }
63: 
64: function parseFrontmatter(content) {
65:   if (!content.startsWith('---')) return null;
66:   const lines = content.split('\n');
67:   if (lines[0].trim() !== '---') return null;
68:   let end = -1;
69:   for (let i = 1; i < lines.length; i += 1) {
70:     if (lines[i].trim() === '---') { end = i; break; }
71:   }
72:   if (end === -1) return null;
73: 
74:   const fields = {};
75:   for (let i = 1; i < end; i += 1) {
76:     const line = lines[i];
77:     const keyMatch = /^([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*):(.*)$/.exec(line);
78:     if (!keyMatch) continue; // indented continuation or list item — handled below
79:     const key = keyMatch[1];
80:     const rest = keyMatch[2];
81: 
82:     if (key === 'trigger_phrases') {
83:       const inline = rest.trim();
84:       if (inline.startsWith('[')) {
85:         const body = inline.replace(/^\[/, '').replace(/\]\s*$/, '');
86:         fields.trigger_phrases = body
87:           .split(',')
88:           .map((item) => cleanScalar(item))
89:           .filter((item) => item.length > 0);
90:       } else {
91:         const items = [];
92:         for (let j = i + 1; j < end; j += 1) {
93:           const itemMatch = /^\s+-\s*(.*)$/.exec(lines[j]);
94:           if (!itemMatch) break;
95:           const item = cleanScalar(itemMatch[1]);
96:           if (item.length > 0) items.push(item);
97:         }
98:         fields.trigger_phrases = items;
99:       }
100:     } else {
101:       fields[key] = cleanScalar(rest);
102:     }
103:   }
104:   return fields;
105: }
106: 
107: function validateBlock(fields) {
108:   const problems = [];
109:   if (!fields.title) problems.push('title missing or empty');
110:   if (!fields.description) problems.push('description missing or empty');
111:   const phrases = fields.trigger_phrases;
112:   if (!Array.isArray(phrases) || phrases.length === 0) {
113:     problems.push('trigger_phrases missing or empty');
114:   } else if (phrases.length < 3 || phrases.length > 8) {
115:     problems.push(`trigger_phrases must have 3-8 items (found ${phrases.length})`);
116:   }
117:   if (!fields.importance_tier) {
118:     problems.push('importance_tier missing');
119:   } else if (!IMPORTANCE_TIERS.has(fields.importance_tier)) {
120:     problems.push(`importance_tier "${fields.importance_tier}" not in {${[...IMPORTANCE_TIERS].join('|')}}`);
121:   }
122:   if (!fields.contextType) {
123:     problems.push('contextType missing');
124:   } else if (!CONTEXT_TYPES.has(fields.contextType)) {
125:     problems.push(`contextType "${fields.contextType}" not in {${[...CONTEXT_TYPES].join('|')}}`);
126:   }
127:   return problems;
128: }
129: 
130: function listMarkdownFiles(dir) {
131:   let entries;
132:   try {
133:     entries = readdirSync(dir, { withFileTypes: true });
134:   } catch {
135:     return [];
136:   }
137:   const files = [];
138:   for (const entry of entries) {
139:     const full = join(dir, entry.name);
140:     if (entry.isDirectory()) files.push(...listMarkdownFiles(full));
141:     else if (entry.isFile() && entry.name.toLowerCase().endsWith('.md')) files.push(full);
142:   }
143:   return files;
144: }
145: 
146: const { root, mode, skill } = parseArgs(process.argv.slice(2));
147: const skillsRoot = join(root, '.opencode', 'skills');
148: 
149: let skillDirs;
150: try {
151:   skillDirs = readdirSync(skillsRoot, { withFileTypes: true })
152:     .filter((entry) => entry.isDirectory())
153:     .map((entry) => entry.name)
154:     .sort();
155: } catch {
156:   process.stderr.write(`skills root not found: ${skillsRoot}\n`);
157:   process.exit(2);
158: }
159: if (skill) {
160:   if (!skillDirs.includes(skill)) {
161:     process.stderr.write(`unknown skill: ${skill}\n`);
162:     process.exit(2);
163:   }
164:   skillDirs = [skill];
165: }
166: 
167: let checked = 0;
168: let authored = 0;
169: let failures = 0;
170: 
171: for (const skillName of skillDirs) {
172:   for (const subdir of DOC_SUBDIRS) {
173:     for (const file of listMarkdownFiles(join(skillsRoot, skillName, subdir))) {
174:       if (basename(file).toLowerCase() === 'readme.md') continue;
175:       checked += 1;
176:       const relPath = relative(root, file);
177:       const fields = parseFrontmatter(readFileSync(file, 'utf8'));
178:       const carriesDetailed =
179:         fields !== null && DETAILED_FIELDS.some((key) => key in fields);
180:       if (carriesDetailed) authored += 1;
181: 
182:       if (mode === 'shape' && !carriesDetailed) continue;
183:       if (mode === 'coverage' && fields === null) {
184:         failures += 1;
185:         process.stdout.write(`FAIL ${relPath}: no leading frontmatter block\n`);
186:         continue;
187:       }
188: 
189:       const problems = validateBlock(fields ?? {});
190:       if (problems.length > 0) {
191:         failures += 1;
192:         process.stdout.write(`FAIL ${relPath}: ${problems.join('; ')}\n`);
193:       }
194:     }
195:   }
196: }
197: 
198: process.stdout.write(
199:   `${failures === 0 ? 'PASS' : 'FAIL'} mode=${mode} scope=${skill ?? 'all-skills'} ` +
200:   `docs=${checked} carrying-detailed-block=${authored} violations=${failures}\n`,
201: );
202: process.exit(failures === 0 ? 0 : 1);

(End of file - total 202 lines)
</content>

63: export type AdvisorHookStatus = AdvisorEnvelopeStatus;

65: 
66: export interface SkillAdvisorBriefOptions {

77:   readonly errorClass?: AdvisorErrorClass;

93: 
94: export interface AdvisorHookResult {
95:   readonly status: AdvisorHookStatus;
96:   readonly freshness: AdvisorHookFreshness;
97:   readonly brief: string | null;
98:   readonly recommendations: AdvisorRecommendation[];
99:   readonly diagnostics: AdvisorHookDiagnostics | null;
100:   readonly metrics: AdvisorHookMetrics;
101:   readonly generatedAt: string;
102:   readonly sharedPayload: SharedPayloadEnvelope | null;
103: }
104: 
105: interface CachedAdvisorHookResult extends AdvisorHookResult {

108: }
109: 
110: const DEFAULT_TOKEN_CAP = 80;
111: const HARD_TOKEN_CAP = 120;
112: export const DEFAULT_ADVISOR_CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD = resolvedConfidenceThreshold();
113: export const DEFAULT_ADVISOR_UNCERTAINTY_THRESHOLD = resolvedUncertaintyThreshold();
114: const DEFAULT_METRICS: AdvisorHookMetrics = {
115:   durationMs: 0,
116:   cacheHit: false,
117:   subprocessInvoked: false,
118:   retriesAttempted: 0,

123: export interface ResolvedAdvisorThresholdConfig {
124:   readonly confidenceThreshold: number;

128:   readonly includeAttribution: boolean;
129:   readonly includeAbstainReasons: boolean;

132: function clampTokenCap(maxTokens: number | undefined, ambiguous: boolean): number {
133:   const requested = maxTokens ?? (ambiguous ? HARD_TOKEN_CAP : DEFAULT_TOKEN_CAP);
134:   return Math.min(Math.max(1, Math.floor(requested)), HARD_TOKEN_CAP);
135: }
136: 
137: export function resolveAdvisorThresholdConfig(
138:   thresholdConfig: AdvisorThresholds | undefined,
139: ): ResolvedAdvisorThresholdConfig {
140:   return {
141:     confidenceThreshold: thresholdConfig?.confidenceThreshold ?? DEFAULT_ADVISOR_CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD,
142:     uncertaintyThreshold: thresholdConfig?.uncertaintyThreshold ?? DEFAULT_ADVISOR_UNCERTAINTY_THRESHOLD,
143:     confidenceOnly: thresholdConfig?.confidenceOnly ?? false,
144:     showRejections: thresholdConfig?.showRejections ?? false,
145:     includeAttribution: thresholdConfig?.includeAttribution ?? false,
146:     includeAbstainReasons: thresholdConfig?.includeAbstainReasons ?? false,
147:   };

149: 
150: export function passingRecommendations(
151:   recommendations: readonly AdvisorRecommendation[],
152:   thresholdConfig: AdvisorThresholds | undefined,
153: ): AdvisorRecommendation[] {
154:   const resolvedThresholds = resolveAdvisorThresholdConfig(thresholdConfig);
155:   return recommendations.filter((recommendation) => {
156:     if (recommendation.passes_threshold === true) {
157:       return true;
158:     }
159:     if (resolvedThresholds.confidenceOnly) {
160:       return recommendation.confidence >= resolvedThresholds.confidenceThreshold;
161:     }
162:     return recommendation.confidence >= resolvedThresholds.confidenceThreshold
163:       && recommendation.uncertainty <= resolvedThresholds.uncertaintyThreshold;
164:   });
165: }
166: 
167: function renderSharedBrief(
168:   recommendations: readonly AdvisorRecommendation[],

171:   thresholdConfig: AdvisorThresholds | undefined,
172: ): string | null {
173:   const renderableResult: AdvisorBriefRenderableResult = {

175:     freshness,
176:     recommendations,

178:     sharedPayload: null,

185: 
186: export function buildAdvisorHookResultFromRecommendations(args: {

1: #!/usr/bin/env bash
2: # ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
3: # SCRIPT: verify-zombie-soak.sh
4: # ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
5: # Verifies SC-002 (zero-zombie soak) for the three MCP launchers
6: # shipped in packets 006-skill-advisor/006-009. Run after the new
7: # launcher binaries have been live for ≥ 24 hours under normal
8: # multi-runtime use.
9: #
10: # Exit 0 = clean (≤ 1 launcher per server, no excess).
11: # Exit 1 = duplicates found (zombie state — investigate).
12: #
13: # Usage:
14: #   bash .opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/scripts/verify-zombie-soak.sh
15: #   bash .opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/scripts/verify-zombie-soak.sh --verbose
16: #
17: # Evidence capture:
18: #   bash .../verify-zombie-soak.sh --verbose > /tmp/soak-evidence-$(date +%Y%m%d).log 2>&1
19: 
20: set -euo pipefail
21: 
22: VERBOSE=0
23: case "${1:-}" in
24:   --verbose|-v) VERBOSE=1 ;;
25: esac
26: 
27: ts() { date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"; }
28: 
29: count_launcher() {
30:   local pattern="$1"
31:   ps -eo command | grep -E "^node .*${pattern}" | grep -v grep | wc -l | tr -d '[:space:]'
32: }
33: 
34: count_child_context_server() {
35:   ps -eo command | grep -E "node.*context-server\.js" | grep -v grep | wc -l | tr -d '[:space:]'
36: }
37: 
38: list_launcher_pids() {
39:   local pattern="$1"
40:   ps -eo pid,etime,command | grep -E "^[[:space:]]*[0-9]+.*node .*${pattern}" | grep -v grep
41: }
42: 
43: SKILL_ADVISOR_COUNT=$(count_launcher "mk-skill-advisor-launcher")
44: CODE_INDEX_COUNT=$(count_launcher "mk-code-index-launcher")
45: SPEC_MEMORY_COUNT=$(count_launcher "mk-spec-memory-launcher")
46: CONTEXT_SERVER_COUNT=$(count_child_context_server)
47: 
48: EXIT=0
49: 
50: printf "=== Zero-Zombie Soak Verification ===\n"
51: printf "Timestamp: %s\n" "$(ts)"
52: printf "Working tree: %s\n" "$(pwd)"
53: printf "\n"
54: printf "Launcher process counts (target: ≤ 1 each):\n"
55: printf "  mk-skill-advisor-launcher: %s\n" "$SKILL_ADVISOR_COUNT"
56: printf "  mk-code-index-launcher:    %s\n" "$CODE_INDEX_COUNT"
57: printf "  mk-spec-memory-launcher:   %s\n" "$SPEC_MEMORY_COUNT"
58: printf "  context-server.js (child): %s\n" "$CONTEXT_SERVER_COUNT"
59: printf "\n"
60: 
61: # Verify each launcher count is at most 1.
62: if [ "$SKILL_ADVISOR_COUNT" -gt 1 ]; then
63:   printf "FAIL: mk-skill-advisor-launcher has %s instances (expected ≤ 1)\n" "$SKILL_ADVISOR_COUNT" >&2
64:   EXIT=1
65: fi
66: if [ "$CODE_INDEX_COUNT" -gt 1 ]; then
67:   printf "FAIL: mk-code-index-launcher has %s instances (expected ≤ 1)\n" "$CODE_INDEX_COUNT" >&2
68:   EXIT=1
69: fi
70: if [ "$SPEC_MEMORY_COUNT" -gt 1 ]; then
71:   printf "FAIL: mk-spec-memory-launcher has %s instances (expected ≤ 1)\n" "$SPEC_MEMORY_COUNT" >&2
72:   EXIT=1
73: fi
74: # context-server.js is the spec-memory child; should match spec-memory launcher count.
75: if [ "$CONTEXT_SERVER_COUNT" -gt 1 ]; then
76:   printf "FAIL: context-server.js has %s instances (expected ≤ 1)\n" "$CONTEXT_SERVER_COUNT" >&2
77:   EXIT=1
78: fi
79: 
80: # Verify any leftover .corrupt files (carry-over hygiene check).
81: CORRUPT_COUNT=$(find .opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/database -name "*.corrupt*" 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d '[:space:]')
82: printf "Quarantine files (.corrupt*) on disk: %s\n" "$CORRUPT_COUNT"
83: if [ "$CORRUPT_COUNT" -gt 0 ]; then
84:   printf "WARN: %s .corrupt* files present — soak window may have produced corruption.\n" "$CORRUPT_COUNT" >&2
85:   if [ "$VERBOSE" -eq 1 ]; then
86:     find .opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/database -name "*.corrupt*" 2>/dev/null
87:   fi
88: fi
89:

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# SCRIPT: verify-zombie-soak.sh
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Verifies SC-002 (zero-zombie soak) for the three MCP launchers
# shipped in packets 006-skill-advisor/006-009. Run after the new
# launcher binaries have been live for ≥ 24 hours under normal
# multi-runtime use.
#
# Exit 0 = clean (≤ 1 launcher per server, no excess).
# Exit 1 = duplicates found (zombie state — investigate).
#
# Usage:
#   bash .opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/scripts/verify-zombie-soak.sh
#   bash .opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/scripts/verify-zombie-soak.sh --verbose
#
# Evidence capture:
#   bash .../verify-zombie-soak.sh --verbose > /tmp/soak-evidence-$(date +%Y%m%d).log 2>&1

set -euo pipefail

display.text:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# SCRIPT: verify-zombie-soak.sh
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Verifies SC-002 (zero-zombie soak) for the three MCP launchers
# shipped in packets 006-skill-advisor/006-009. Run after the new
# launcher binaries have been live for ≥ 24 hours under normal
# multi-runtime use.
#
# Exit 0 = clean (≤ 1 launcher per server, no excess).
# Exit 1 = duplicates found (zombie state — investigate).
#
# Usage:
#   bash .opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/scripts/verify-zombie-soak.sh
#   bash .opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/scripts/verify-zombie-soak.sh --verbose
#
# Evidence capture:
#   bash .../verify-zombie-soak.sh --verbose > /tmp/soak-evidence-$(date +%Y%m%d).log 2>&1

set -euo pipefail

VERBOSE=0
case "${1:-}" in
  --verbose|-v) VERBOSE=1 ;;
esac

ts() { date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"; }

count_launcher() {
  local pattern="$1"
  ps -eo command | grep -E "^node .*${pattern}" | grep -v grep | wc -l | tr -d '[:space:]'
}

count_child_context_server() {
  ps -eo command | grep -E "node.*context-server\.js" | grep -v grep | wc -l | tr -d '[:space:]'
}

list_launcher_pids() {
  local pattern="$1"
  ps -eo pid,etime,command | grep -E "^[[:space:]]*[0-9]+.*node .*${pattern}" | grep -v grep
}

SKILL_ADVISOR_COUNT=$(count_launcher "mk-skill-advisor-launcher")
CODE_INDEX_COUNT=$(count_launcher "mk-code-index-launcher")
SPEC_MEMORY_COUNT=$(count_launcher "mk-spec-memory-launcher")
CONTEXT_SERVER_COUNT=$(count_child_context_server)

EXIT=0

printf "=== Zero-Zombie Soak Verification ===\n"
printf "Timestamp: %s\n" "$(ts)"
printf "Working tree: %s\n" "$(pwd)"
printf "\n"
printf "Launcher process counts (target: ≤ 1 each):\n"
printf "  mk-skill-advisor-launcher: %s\n" "$SKILL_ADVISOR_COUNT"
printf "  mk-code-index-launcher:    %s\n" "$CODE_INDEX_COUNT"
printf "  mk-spec-memory-launcher:   %s\n" "$SPEC_MEMORY_COUNT"
printf "  context-server.js (child): %s\n" "$CONTEXT_SERVER_COUNT"
printf "\n"

# Verify each launcher count is at most 1.
if [ "$SKILL_ADVISOR_COUNT" -gt 1 ]; then
  printf "FAIL: mk-skill-advisor-launcher has %s instances (expected ≤ 1)\n" "$SKILL_ADVISOR_COUNT" >&2
  EXIT=1
fi
if [ "$CODE_INDEX_COUNT" -gt 1 ]; then
  printf "FAIL: mk-code-index-launcher has %s instances (expected ≤ 1)\n" "$CODE_INDEX_COUNT" >&2
  EXIT=1
fi
if [ "$SPEC_MEMORY_COUNT" -gt 1 ]; then
  printf "FAIL: mk-spec-memory-launcher has %s instances (expected ≤ 1)\n" "$SPEC_MEMORY_COUNT" >&2
  EXIT=1
fi
# context-server.js is the spec-memory child; should match spec-memory launcher count.
if [ "$CONTEXT_SERVER_COUNT" -gt 1 ]; then
  printf "FAIL: context-server.js has %s instances (expected ≤ 1)\n" "$CONTEXT_SERVER_COUNT" >&2
  EXIT=1
fi

# Verify any leftover .corrupt files (carry-over hygiene check).
CORRUPT_COUNT=$(find .opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/database -name "*.corrupt*" 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d '[:space:]')
printf "Quarantine files (.corrupt*) on disk: %s\n" "$CORRUPT_COUNT"
if [ "$CORRUPT_COUNT" -gt 0 ]; then
  printf "WARN: %s .corrupt* files present — soak window may have produced corruption.\n" "$CORRUPT_COUNT" >&2
  if [ "$VERBOSE" -eq 1 ]; then
    find .opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/database -name "*.corrupt*" 2>/dev/null
  fi
fi

if [ "$VERBOSE" -eq 1 ]; then
  printf "\n=== Detailed launcher PIDs ===\n"
  printf "\n-- mk-skill-advisor-launcher --\n"
  list_launcher_pids "mk-skill-advisor-launcher" || true
  printf "\n-- mk-code-index-launcher --\n"
  list_launcher_pids "mk-code-index-launcher" || true
  printf "\n-- mk-spec-memory-launcher --\n"
  list_launcher_pids "mk-spec-memory-launcher" || true
fi

printf "\n"
if [ "$EXIT" -eq 0 ]; then
  printf "RESULT: PASS — no zombie launchers detected.\n"
else
  printf "RESULT: FAIL — duplicate launcher(s) present. The 006-skill-advisor lease invariants may be broken; investigate.\n"
fi

exit "$EXIT"

<type>file</type>
<content>
1: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // MODULE: Cross-Skill Edges Apply Patch
3: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4: // Idempotent JSON patching for applying enhance edges to graph-metadata.json.
5: 
6: import { readFile, writeFile } from 'node:fs/promises';
7: import { basename, resolve, sep } from 'node:path';
8: import type { EdgeSourceKind, EdgeWriteIntent, InboundEnhanceCandidate } from './types.js';
9: 
10: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
11: // 1. APPLY EDGE
12: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
13: 
14: const PROTECTED_SOURCE_KINDS = new Set<EdgeSourceKind>(['manual', 'trusted']);
15: 
16: function deriveSourceKind(writeIntent: EdgeWriteIntent): EdgeSourceKind {
17:   return writeIntent === 'trusted-maintainer' ? 'trusted' : 'automated';
18: }
19: 
20: function readSourceKind(edge: Record<string, unknown>): EdgeSourceKind | null {
21:   const value = edge.source_kind;
22:   if (value === 'automated' || value === 'manual' || value === 'trusted') {
23:     return value;
24:   }
25:   return null;
26: }
27: 
28: function buildAutomatedReason(candidate: InboundEnhanceCandidate): string {
29:   return candidate.rules.map(r => `${r.rule}:${r.contribution.toFixed(2)}`).join(' + ');
30: }
31: 
32: /**
33:  * Validate that the source path is a graph-metadata.json file under the trusted skills root.
34:  * Prevents path traversal in apply mode.
35:  */
36: function isPathWithin(candidatePath: string, trustedRoot: string): boolean {
37:   const resolvedCandidate = resolve(candidatePath);
38:   const resolvedRoot = resolve(trustedRoot);
39:   const rootWithSep = resolvedRoot.endsWith(sep) ? resolvedRoot : resolvedRoot + sep;
40:   return resolvedCandidate.startsWith(rootWithSep) && basename(resolvedCandidate) === 'graph-metadata.json';
41: }
42: 
43: /**
44:  * Apply an enhance edge to the source skill's graph-metadata.json.
45:  * Idempotent for automated writes and protects manually maintained provenance.
46:  *
47:  * @param candidate - The candidate to apply
48:  * @param skillsRoot - Optional trusted workspace root. When provided, candidate.sourcePath
49:  *                    MUST resolve under this root and have basename 'graph-metadata.json'.
50:  * This guards against path traversal in apply mode.
51:  */
52: export async function applyEnhanceEdge(
53:   candidate: InboundEnhanceCandidate,
54:   skillsRoot?: string,
55:   writeIntent: EdgeWriteIntent = 'automated',
56: ): Promise<{ applied: boolean; reason: string }> {
57:   if (!candidate.applyable) {
58:     return { applied: false, reason: `not applyable: ${candidate.blockers.join('; ')}` };
59:   }
60: 
61:   if (skillsRoot && !isPathWithin(candidate.sourcePath, skillsRoot)) {
62:     return {
63:       applied: false,
64:       reason: `path-boundary violation: ${candidate.sourcePath} not under ${skillsRoot}`,
65:     };
66:   }
67: 
68:   try {
69:     const raw = await readFile(candidate.sourcePath, 'utf-8');
70:     const parsed = JSON.parse(raw);
71: 
72:     if (!parsed.edges) {
73:       parsed.edges = {};
74:     }
75:     if (!parsed.edges.enhances) {
76:       parsed.edges.enhances = [];
77:     }
78: 
79:     const enhances: Array<Record<string, unknown>> = parsed.edges.enhances;
80:     const sourceKind = deriveSourceKind(writeIntent);
81:     const existingEdge = enhances.find(e => e.target === candidate.targetSkillId);
82: 
83:     if (existingEdge && writeIntent === 'automated') {
84:       const existingSourceKind = readSourceKind(existingEdge);
85:       if (existingSourceKind && PROTECTED_SOURCE_KINDS.has(existingSourceKind)) {
86:         return { applied: false, reason: 'edge already exists: manual provenance protected' };
87:       }
88:       return { applied: false, reason: 'edge already exists' };
89:     }
90: 
91:     if (existingEdge) {
92:       existingEdge.weight = candidate.weight;
93:       existingEdge.context = candidate.context;
94:       existingEdge.source_kind = sourceKind;
95:       // A trusted overwrite supersedes any earlier automated origin; drop the
96:       // automated-provenance markers so the edge cannot carry contradictory
97:       // {source_kind:'trusted', auto_added_reason:...} provenance.
98:       delete existingEdge.auto_added_at;
99:       delete existingEdge.auto_added_reason;
100:       await writeFile(candidate.sourcePath, JSON.stringify(parsed, null, 2) + '\n', 'utf-8');
101:       return { applied: true, reason: 'edge updated by trusted maintainer' };
102:     }
103: 
104:     const newEdge = {
105:       target: candidate.targetSkillId,
106:       weight: candidate.weight,
107:       context: candidate.context,
108:       source_kind: sourceKind,
109:     };
110: 
111:     if (writeIntent === 'automated') {
112:       Object.assign(newEdge, {
113:         auto_added_at: new Date().toISOString(),
114:         auto_added_reason: buildAutomatedReason(candidate),
115:       });
116:     }
117: 
118:     enhances.push(newEdge);
119:     parsed.edges.enhances = enhances;
120: 
121:     await writeFile(candidate.sourcePath, JSON.stringify(parsed, null, 2) + '\n', 'utf-8');
122:     return { applied: true, reason: `edge added with ${sourceKind} provenance` };
123:   } catch (error) {
124:     const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
125:     return { applied: false, reason: `failed to apply edge: ${message}` };
126:   }
127: }

(End of file - total 127 lines)
</content>

170:     // Log error but don't fail entire load
171:     console.warn(`[cross-skill-edges] Failed to parse ${sourcePath}:`, error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error));
172:     return null;
173:   }
174: }
175: 
176: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
177: // 3. PUBLIC API
178: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
179: 
180: export interface LoadSkillMetadataResult {
181:   records: SkillMetadataRecord[];
182:   errors: Array<{ skillId: string; error: string }>;
183: }
184: 
185: /**
186:  * Load all skill metadata files from the skills root directory.
187:  * Returns an array of SkillMetadataRecord objects.
188:  */
189: export async function loadAllSkillMetadata(skillsRoot: string): Promise<SkillMetadataRecord[]> {
190:   const { records } = await loadAllSkillMetadataWithErrors(skillsRoot);
191:   return records;
192: }
193: 
194: /**
195:  * Load all skill metadata files and surface per-file parse errors.
196:  * Used by propagateInboundEnhances to populate PropagateEnhancesResult.errors[].
197:  */
198: export async function loadAllSkillMetadataWithErrors(skillsRoot: string): Promise<LoadSkillMetadataResult> {
199:   const absoluteRoot = resolve(skillsRoot);
200:   const discoveredFiles = discoverGraphMetadataFiles(absoluteRoot);
201:   const records: SkillMetadataRecord[] = [];
202:   const errors: Array<{ skillId: string; error: string }> = [];
203: 
204:   for (const sourcePath of discoveredFiles) {
205:     const result = parseSkillMetadataWithError(sourcePath);
206:     if (result.record) {
207:       records.push(result.record);
208:     } else if (result.error) {
209:       errors.push({ skillId: basename(dirname(sourcePath)), error: result.error });
210:     }
211:   }
212: 
213:   return { records, errors };
214: }
215: 
216: /**
217:  * Parse a single graph-metadata.json file with explicit error surfacing.
218:  * Returns either a record (success), an error string (parse/validation failure),
219:  * or both null (file was a non-skill JSON e.g. test fixture — silently skipped).
220:  */
221: function parseSkillMetadataWithError(sourcePath: string): { record: SkillMetadataRecord | null; error: string | null } {
222:   try {
223:     const content = readFileSync(sourcePath, 'utf8');
224:     const parsedJson = JSON.parse(content) as unknown;
225: 
226:     if (!isRecord(parsedJson)) {
227:       return { record: null, error: null };
228:     }
229:     const hasSkillFields = typeof parsedJson.skill_id === 'string' ||
230:                           typeof parsedJson.family === 'string' ||
231:                           isRecord(parsedJson.edges);
232:     if (!hasSkillFields) {
233:       return { record: null, error: null };
234:     }
235: 
236:     const record = parseSkillMetadata(sourcePath);
237:     return { record, error: record ? null : 'parse failed (see console.warn for details)' };
238:   } catch (error) {
239:     const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
240:     return { record: null, error: message };
241:   }
242: }
243: 
244: /**
245:  * Group skill metadata records by family.
246:  * Returns a Map from family name to array of records.
247:  */
248: export function groupByFamily(records: SkillMetadataRecord[]): Map<string, SkillMetadataRecord[]> {
249:   const byFamily = new Map<string, SkillMetadataRecord[]>();
250: 
251:   for (const record of records) {
252:     const family = record.family;
253:     const familyRecords = byFamily.get(family) ?? [];
254:     familyRecords.push(record);
255:     byFamily.set(family, familyRecords);
256:   }
257: 
258:   return byFamily;
259: }

<content>
1: #!/usr/bin/env python3
2: # ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
3: # COMPONENT: SKILL GRAPH COMPILER
4: # ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
5: 
6: """
7: Skill Graph Compiler - Scans skill folders for graph-metadata.json,
8: validates schema, and compiles into a single skill-graph.json.
9: 
10: Usage:
11:     python skill_graph_compiler.py                    # Validate + compile in memory
12:     python skill_graph_compiler.py --validate-only    # Validate without writing
13:     python skill_graph_compiler.py --export-json      # Write compiled JSON to default output
14:     python skill_graph_compiler.py --pretty           # Human-readable output
15:     python skill_graph_compiler.py --output PATH      # Custom output path
16: """
17: 
18: import argparse
19: import json
20: import os
21: import re
22: import sys
23: from datetime import datetime, timezone
24: from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional, Set, Tuple
25: 
26: 
27: # ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
28: # 1. CONFIGURATION
29: # ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
30: 
31: SCRIPT_DIR = os.path.dirname(os.path.realpath(__file__))
32: SKILLS_DIR = os.path.abspath(os.path.join(SCRIPT_DIR, "..", "..", ".."))
33: DEFAULT_OUTPUT = os.path.join(SCRIPT_DIR, "skill-graph.json")
34: 
35: COMPILED_SCHEMA_VERSION = 1
36: ALLOWED_METADATA_SCHEMA_VERSIONS = {1, 2}
37: 
38: ALLOWED_FAMILIES = {"cli", "mcp", "sk-code", "deep-loop", "sk-util", "system"}
39: ALLOWED_CATEGORIES = {
40:     "cli-orchestrator", "mcp-tool", "code-quality",
41:     "autonomous-loop", "utility", "system",
42:     "workflow", "design",
43: }
44: EDGE_TYPES = {"depends_on", "enhances", "siblings", "conflicts_with", "prerequisite_for"}
45: ALLOWED_ENTITY_KINDS = {"skill", "agent", "script", "config", "reference"}
46: # Contract decision: affordance derived inputs MAY declare
47: # routing-direction edges (`depends_on`, `enhances`, `siblings`,
48: # `prerequisite_for`) but MUST NOT declare `conflicts_with`.
49: #
50: # Conflict semantics require authoritative reciprocal declarations
51: # in `edges.conflicts_with` (validated by `validate_edge_symmetry`),
52: # not derived/inferred affordance lanes. Allowing affordances to
53: # inject conflict edges would (a) bypass the bilateral-declaration
54: # invariant and (b) silently no-op anyway in `compile_graph` —
55: # either path violates the explicitness rule.
56: #
57: # Therefore we VALIDATE: any `conflicts_with`/`conflictsWith` field
58: # on an affordance is reported as `derived.affordances[i] has
59: # unsupported field(s)` by `validate_derived_affordances`. This
60: # Closes with an explicit (validate, do not serialize)
61: # contract that mirrors `derived.entities` constraints.
62: AFFORDANCE_RELATION_FIELDS = {
63:     "dependsOn": "depends_on",
64:     "depends_on": "depends_on",
65:     "enhances": "enhances",
66:     "siblings": "siblings",
67:     "prerequisiteFor": "prerequisite_for",
68:     "prerequisite_for": "prerequisite_for",
69: }
70: # Reserved (rejected by validation) — kept here so the key set is
71: # explicit at the call site rather than implicit through a missing
72: # entry. Listed in `AFFORDANCE_REJECTED_RELATION_FIELDS` below.
73: AFFORDANCE_REJECTED_RELATION_FIELDS = frozenset({
74:     "conflictsWith",
75:     "conflicts_with",
76: })
77: AFFORDANCE_ALLOWED_FIELDS = {
78:     "skillId", "skill_id", "name", "triggers", "category",
79:     *AFFORDANCE_RELATION_FIELDS.keys(),
80: }
81: # Broadened prompt-injection denylist. Mirrors the TS-side
82: # `INSTRUCTION_PATTERN` in `affordance-normalizer.ts` so both compile-
83: # time (Python) and score-time (TypeScript) input paths reject the
84: # same set of injection-shaped phrases. Real-world corpora include
85: # synonyms (disregard/override/forget), directional variants
86: # (above/prior/earlier/all/any), reveal-prompt probes, role-prefix
87: # variants (user:/human:), and bracketed/angled role markers
88: # (`[INST]`, `<system>`). Anchoring matches the TS pattern: keyword
89: # branches use `(?:^|\s|\b)` so legitimate phrases like "ignore the
90: # cache" or "system call audit" pass.
91: AFFORDANCE_INSTRUCTION_PATTERN = re.compile(
92:     r"(?:"
93:     r"(?:^|\s|\b)(?:"
94:     r"(?:ignore|disregard|forget|skip|bypass|override)\s+"
95:     r"(?:the\s+)?(?:previous|prior|earlier|above|all|any)"
96:     r"(?:\s+(?:and\s+)?(?:previous|prior|earlier|above|all|any))*\s+"
97:     r"(?:instruction|instructions|prompt|prompts|directions?|"
98:     r"rule|rules|context|guidance|message|messages|system\s+prompt)|"
99:     r"override\s+system\s+(?:prompt|message|instructions)|"
100:     r"new\s+instructions?\s*:|"
101:     r"reveal\s+(?:the\s+)?(?:system|developer)\s+"
102:     r"(?:prompt|instruction|instructions)|"
103:     r"system\s*:|developer\s*:|assistant\s*:|user\s*:|human\s*:|"
104:     r"execute\s*:|instruction\s*:"
105:     r")|"
106:     r"\[\s*(?:system|developer|assistant|user|human|inst|instruction)\s*\]|"
107:     r"<\s*(?:system|developer|assistant|user|human)\s*>"
108:     r")",
109:     re.IGNORECASE,
110: )
111: AFFORDANCE_EMAIL_PATTERN = re.compile(r"\b[A-Z0-9._%+-]+@[A-Z0-9.-]+\.[A-Z]{2,}\b", re.IGNORECASE)
112: AFFORDANCE_URL_PATTERN = re.compile(r"\bhttps?://\S+|\bwww\.\S+", re.IGNORECASE)
113: AFFORDANCE_TOKEN_PATTERN = re.compile(r"\b(?:bearer|token|secret|apikey|api_key)\s*[:=]\s*\S+", re.IGNORECASE)
114: 
115: 
116: # ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
117: # Affordance normalization debug counters
118: # ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
119: # Mirror of the TS-side counters in
120: # `skill_advisor/lib/affordance-normalizer.ts`. Operators can read these via
121: # `get_affordance_normalizer_counters()` to diagnose why affordance evidence
122: # does or does not reach the compiled graph. Counters are monotonic across
123: # the lifetime of the process; tests may call
124: # `reset_affordance_normalizer_counters()` between cases.

display.text:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# COMPONENT: SKILL GRAPH COMPILER
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"""
Skill Graph Compiler - Scans skill folders for graph-metadata.json,
validates schema, and compiles into a single skill-graph.json.

Usage:
    python skill_graph_compiler.py                    # Validate + compile in memory
    python skill_graph_compiler.py --validate-only    # Validate without writing
    python skill_graph_compiler.py --export-json      # Write compiled JSON to default output
    python skill_graph_compiler.py --pretty           # Human-readable output
    python skill_graph_compiler.py --output PATH      # Custom output path
"""

import argparse
import json
import os
import re
import sys
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional, Set, Tuple


# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# 1. CONFIGURATION
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

SCRIPT_DIR = os.path.dirname(os.path.realpath(__file__))
SKILLS_DIR = os.path.abspath(os.path.join(SCRIPT_DIR, "..", "..", ".."))
DEFAULT_OUTPUT = os.path.join(SCRIPT_DIR, "skill-graph.json")

COMPILED_SCHEMA_VERSION = 1
ALLOWED_METADATA_SCHEMA_VERSIONS = {1, 2}

ALLOWED_FAMILIES = {"cli", "mcp", "sk-code", "deep-loop", "sk-util", "system"}
ALLOWED_CATEGORIES = {
    "cli-orchestrator", "mcp-tool", "code-quality",
    "autonomous-loop", "utility", "system",
    "workflow", "design",
}
EDGE_TYPES = {"depends_on", "enhances", "siblings", "conflicts_with", "prerequisite_for"}
ALLOWED_ENTITY_KINDS = {"skill", "agent", "script", "config", "reference"}
# Contract decision: affordance derived inputs MAY declare
# routing-direction edges (`depends_on`, `enhances`, `siblings`,
# `prerequisite_for`) but MUST NOT declare `conflicts_with`.
#
# Conflict semantics require authoritative reciprocal declarations
# in `edges.conflicts_with` (validated by `validate_edge_symmetry`),
# not derived/inferred affordance lanes. Allowing affordances to
# inject conflict edges would (a) bypass the bilateral-declaration
# invariant and (b) silently no-op anyway in `compile_graph` —
# either path violates the explicitness rule.
#
# Therefore we VALIDATE: any `conflicts_with`/`conflictsWith` field
# on an affordance is reported as `derived.affordances[i] has
# unsupported field(s)` by `validate_derived_affordances`. This
# Closes with an explicit (validate, do not serialize)
# contract that mirrors `derived.entities` constraints.
AFFORDANCE_RELATION_FIELDS = {
    "dependsOn": "depends_on",
    "depends_on": "depends_on",
    "enhances": "enhances",
    "siblings": "siblings",
    "prerequisiteFor": "prerequisite_for",
    "prerequisite_for": "prerequisite_for",
}
# Reserved (rejected by validation) — kept here so the key set is
# explicit at the call site rather than implicit through a missing
# entry. Listed in `AFFORDANCE_REJECTED_RELATION_FIELDS` below.
AFFORDANCE_REJECTED_RELATION_FIELDS = frozenset({
    "conflictsWith",
    "conflicts_with",
})
AFFORDANCE_ALLOWED_FIELDS = {
    "skillId", "skill_id", "name", "triggers", "category",
    *AFFORDANCE_RELATION_FIELDS.keys(),
}
# Broadened prompt-injection denylist. Mirrors the TS-side
# `INSTRUCTION_PATTERN` in `affordance-normalizer.ts` so both compile-
# time (Python) and score-time (TypeScript) input paths reject the
# same set of injection-shaped phrases. Real-world corpora include
# synonyms (disregard/override/forget), directional variants
# (above/prior/earlier/all/any), reveal-prompt probes, role-prefix
# variants (user:/human:), and bracketed/angled role markers
# (`[INST]`, `<system>`). Anchoring matches the TS pattern: keyword
# branches use `(?:^|\s|\b)` so legitimate phrases like "ignore the
# cache" or "system call audit" pass.
AFFORDANCE_INSTRUCTION_PATTERN = re.compile(
    r"(?:"
    r"(?:^|\s|\b)(?:"
    r"(?:ignore|disregard|forget|skip|bypass|override)\s+"
    r"(?:the\s+)?(?:previous|prior|earlier|above|all|any)"
    r"(?:\s+(?:and\s+)?(?:previous|prior|earlier|above|all|any))*\s+"
    r"(?:instruction|instructions|prompt|prompts|directions?|"
    r"rule|rules|context|guidance|message|messages|system\s+prompt)|"
    r"override\s+system\s+(?:prompt|message|instructions)|"
    r"new\s+instructions?\s*:|"
    r"reveal\s+(?:the\s+)?(?:system|developer)\s+"
    r"(?:prompt|instruction|instructions)|"
    r"system\s*:|developer\s*:|assistant\s*:|user\s*:|human\s*:|"
    r"execute\s*:|instruction\s*:"
    r")|"
    r"\[\s*(?:system|developer|assistant|user|human|inst|instruction)\s*\]|"
    r"<\s*(?:system|developer|assistant|user|human)\s*>"
    r")",
    re.IGNORECASE,
)
AFFORDANCE_EMAIL_PATTERN = re.compile(r"\b[A-Z0-9._%+-]+@[A-Z0-9.-]+\.[A-Z]{2,}\b", re.IGNORECASE)
AFFORDANCE_URL_PATTERN = re.compile(r"\bhttps?://\S+|\bwww\.\S+", re.IGNORECASE)
AFFORDANCE_TOKEN_PATTERN = re.compile(r"\b(?:bearer|token|secret|apikey|api_key)\s*[:=]\s*\S+", re.IGNORECASE)


# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Affordance normalization debug counters
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Mirror of the TS-side counters in
# `skill_advisor/lib/affordance-normalizer.ts`. Operators can read these via
# `get_affordance_normalizer_counters()` to diagnose why affordance evidence
# does or does not reach the compiled graph. Counters are monotonic across
# the lifetime of the process; tests may call
# `reset_affordance_normalizer_counters()` between cases.
#
# Drop category semantics:
#   - `received`             : raw affordance inputs handed to
#                              `normalize_affordance_input()`
#   - `accepted`             : inputs that produced a normalized affordance
#   - `dropped_unknown_skill`: skillId missing or scrubbed to empty
#   - `dropped_unsafe`       : retained for future prompt-injection / sanitizer
#                              drop tracking (currently unused because the
#                              sanitizer scrubs at the phrase level rather
#                              than rejecting whole inputs)
#   - `dropped_empty`        : skillId resolved but no triggers + no edges

AFFORDANCE_NORMALIZER_COUNTERS: Dict[str, int] = {
    "received": 0,
    "accepted": 0,
    "dropped_unsafe": 0,
    "dropped_empty": 0,
    "dropped_unknown_skill": 0,
}


def get_affordance_normalizer_counters() -> Dict[str, int]:
    """Return a copy of the affordance-normalizer drop counters."""
    return dict(AFFORDANCE_NORMALIZER_COUNTERS)


def reset_affordance_normalizer_counters() -> None:
    """Reset the affordance-normalizer drop counters (test helper)."""
    for key in AFFORDANCE_NORMALIZER_COUNTERS:
        AFFORDANCE_NORMALIZER_COUNTERS[key] = 0


# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# 2. DISCOVERY
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def discover_graph_metadata(skills_dir: str) -> List[Tuple[str, str, dict]]:
    """Scan skill folders for graph-metadata.json files.

    Returns list of (skill_folder_name, file_path, parsed_json) tuples.
    Skills without graph-metadata.json are silently skipped.

    Raises:
        RuntimeError: If any graph-metadata.json file is corrupt or unreadable,
            to prevent downstream consumers from operating on an incomplete graph.
    """
    results = []
    corrupt: List[Tuple[str, str]] = []

    if not os.path.isdir(skills_dir):
        return results

    for entry in sorted(os.listdir(skills_dir)):
        meta_path = os.path.join(skills_dir, entry, "graph-metadata.json")
        if not os.path.isfile(meta_path):
            continue

        # Skip the scripts directory itself
        if entry == "scripts":
            continue

        try:
            with open(meta_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
                data = json.load(f)
            results.append((entry, meta_path, data))
        except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError) as exc:
            corrupt.append((meta_path, str(exc)))

    advisor_meta_path = os.path.join(
        skills_dir,
        "system-skill-advisor",
        "graph-metadata.json",
    )
    if os.path.isfile(advisor_meta_path) and not any(folder == "system-skill-advisor" for folder, _, _ in results):
        try:
            with open(advisor_meta_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
                data = json.load(f)
            results.append(("system-skill-advisor", advisor_meta_path, data))
        except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError) as exc:
            corrupt.append((advisor_meta_path, str(exc)))

    if corrupt:
        details = "; ".join(f"{path}: {err}" for path, err in corrupt)
        raise RuntimeError(
            f"Corrupt or unreadable graph-metadata.json files detected "
            f"({len(corrupt)} file(s)): {details}"
        )

    return results


# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# 3. VALIDATION
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def validate_skill_metadata(
    folder_name: str,
    data: dict,
    all_skill_ids: Set[str],
) -> List[str]:
    """Validate a single skill's graph-metadata.json against the schema.

    Returns list of error messages (empty = valid).
    """
    errors = []

    # Required top-level fields
    schema_version = data.get("schema_version")
    if schema_version not in ALLOWED_METADATA_SCHEMA_VERSIONS:
        errors.append(
            "schema_version must be one of "
            f"{sorted(ALLOWED_METADATA_SCHEMA_VERSIONS)}, got {schema_version}"
        )

    skill_id = data.get("skill_id")
    if not skill_id:
        errors.append("missing required field: skill_id")
    elif skill_id != folder_name:
        errors.append(f"skill_id '{skill_id}' does not match folder name '{folder_name}'")

    family = data.get("family")
    if not family:
        errors.append("missing required field: family")
    elif family not in ALLOWED_FAMILIES:
        errors.append(f"family '{family}' not in allowed set: {sorted(ALLOWED_FAMILIES)}")

    category = data.get("category")
    if not category:
        errors.append("missing required field: category")
    elif category not in ALLOWED_CATEGORIES:
        errors.append(f"category '{category}' not in allowed set: {sorted(ALLOWED_CATEGORIES)}")

    # Edges validation
    edges = data.get("edges")
    if not isinstance(edges, dict):
        errors.append("missing or invalid 'edges' object")

188: export function createDebouncedCorpusUpdater(

196:   let lastStats: CorpusStats | null = null;
197: 
198:   function flush(): CorpusStats | null {
199:     if (!pendingDocuments) return lastStats;
200:     if (timer) {
201:       clearTimeout(timer);
202:       timer = null;
203:     }
204:     lastStats = cacheOptions
205:       ? computeCorpusStatsCached(pendingDocuments, { ...cacheOptions, now: now() }).stats
206:       : computeCorpusStats(pendingDocuments, now());
207:     pendingDocuments = null;
208:     callback(lastStats);

213:     schedule(documents) {
214:       pendingDocuments = documents;
215:       if (timer) clearTimeout(timer);
216:       timer = setTimeout(flush, debounceMs);
217:       timer.unref?.();
218:     },
